findings.suggested_edit: Before writing code, the executor should: (a) confirm which dispatcher file is the correct target (post-bash.sh vs post-all.sh) by grepping the repo; (b) verify suggestion-record.sh exists and document its expected calling convention (args, exit codes); (c) link to or quote the SUGGESTION: sentinel format spec; (d) confirm the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN dispatcher pattern is present and show the hook-registration mechanism.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; no other files mentioned
findings.finding: The edit set is partially specified. The plan names one dispatcher file (with uncertainty between two candidates) and one helper (suggestion-record.sh), but omits: (1) any hook-registration file or manifest that must be updated to wire the new PostToolUse handler into the Claude hooks system — if there is a hooks config (e.g. .claude/hooks.json or similar), it is not mentioned; (2) any documentation or changelog files required by project conventions; (3) whether post-bash.sh vs post-all.sh constitutes a scope difference (post-all.sh would affect all tool types, not just Bash, which may unintentionally widen scope). The blast radius of 'all Agent tool returns' is noted conceptually but no downstream consumers of suggestion-record.sh are listed to confirm they can handle the new calls.
findings.suggested_edit: Name the exact file (post-bash.sh OR post-all.sh, not both). List any hook manifest/config that requires updating. If post-all.sh is chosen, explicitly acknowledge the wider blast radius and confirm non-Agent tool calls will be short-circuited at the top of the handler.

findings.location: Summary vs Acceptance Criteria; 'Testing Mode: RED' vs AC ordering
findings.finding: Two internal tensions: (1) The plan is in RED mode (tests must be written to fail first, then implementation makes them pass), but the AC lists 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' as the first criterion — implying green state — without explicitly sequencing the RED→GREEN steps. The executor could write implementation first and tests after, violating the RED contract. (2) The Summary says 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' but the AC says 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' without resolving which file; these two sections do not converge on a single target, leaving the executor with an unresolved decision that the AC should lock down.
findings.suggested_edit: (1) Add a sequencing note: 'Write failing tests first (RED), confirm they fail, then implement to make them pass (GREEN), then verify run-all.sh passes.' (2) Resolve the post-bash.sh vs post-all.sh ambiguity in one place and reference that decision consistently in both Summary and AC.

findings.location: AC: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' and 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The primary measurability concern is the 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' criterion, which is qualitative. It does not specify which edge cases are required (e.g., missing SUGGESTION: label, multiple SUGGESTION: lines, empty payload, malformed payload, non-Agent tool returns). An agent could write a single trivial test and technically satisfy this criterion. The run-all.sh verification command is concrete and automatable, which is good. The story context references an external script (.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4) that is not inlined, creating a risk that the agent cannot retrieve it, blocking completability.
findings.suggested_edit: Enumerate the required edge cases explicitly, e.g.: 'Tests must cover: (a) no SUGGESTION: line present, (b) exactly one SUGGESTION: line (happy path), (c) multiple SUGGESTION: lines (only first is recorded), (d) malformed sentinel (no crash + warning emitted), (e) non-Agent tool return (hook is a no-op).'

findings.location: Plan body vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.finding: 1. The plan body mentions 'integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' but no acceptance criterion verifies the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern is correctly implemented — only that the hook is 'registered in consolidated dispatcher.' These are distinct requirements (registration vs. correct pattern usage). 2. The plan body names two candidate files ('post-bash.sh or post-all.sh') without deciding which one; the AC says only 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' which does not resolve the ambiguity or verify the correct file was chosen. 3. 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' in the AC is covered in the summary, so that mapping is fine. No orphan criteria are present, but the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern mention in the description has no corresponding criterion.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC such as: 'Hook follows the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN dispatcher pattern (e.g., correct exit-code/signal handling).' Also resolve the post-bash.sh vs. post-all.sh choice in the description rather than leaving it open.

findings.location: Plan body: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return'; 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern'
findings.finding: Three decision points are left open for the executor: (1) Which file to modify — 'post-bash.sh or post-all.sh' is an explicit either/or with no resolution criteria given. (2) What constitutes a 'malformed' sentinel is undefined — no format spec for a well-formed SUGGESTION: line is provided anywhere in the plan, so the agent cannot know what to warn about. (3) 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern' is referenced without any definition or pointer to documentation; an agent unfamiliar with this codebase will have to infer it. The external story context (.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4) may contain this information but is not inlined.
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Specify the target file (e.g., 'post-all.sh because it covers all tool types'). 2. Define the expected sentinel format inline (e.g., 'SUGGESTION: <non-empty text on the same line'). 3. Either inline the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern definition or add a direct link to documentation.

findings.location: AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'; 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes'
findings.finding: The run-all.sh command gives a clear binary termination signal. However, the gap between 'what we'll do' and 'done' is that there is no criterion verifying that suggestion-record.sh is actually called with the correct arguments when a SUGGESTION: line is found. The AC says the hook 'calls suggestion-record.sh' but there is no test criterion that asserts the arguments passed are correct (e.g., correct sentinel text is passed). Additionally, since run-all.sh is referenced but not defined in this plan, it is ambiguous whether it will include the new tests or requires manual registration.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC: 'Tests assert suggestion-record.sh is invoked with the extracted sentinel text as argument when a valid SUGGESTION: line is present.' Clarify whether new test files are automatically discovered by run-all.sh or must be explicitly registered.

findings.location: Context section and Summary
Title vs. Summary and Acceptance Criteria
findings.finding: The title ('Implement PostToolUse hook to extract SUGGESTION: sentinel from sub-agent returns') is faithfully reflected in the summary and all acceptance criteria. No scope drift is observed — the plan does not add unrelated features, and the described work (hook, extraction, warning, registration, tests) is fully consistent with the headline intent.

findings.location: AC: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes'
findings.finding: The first AC ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') is mechanically measurable and automatable. However, 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' (last AC) is under-specified: 'edge cases' is not enumerated, so it is unclear when coverage is sufficient. An agent could add a single trivial test and claim the criterion is met. Similarly, 'Warns on malformed sentinel' has no definition of what 'malformed' means (missing value? wrong prefix format? extra colons?), making it hard to verify exhaustively in-session.
findings.suggested_edit: Enumerate the edge cases explicitly, e.g.: (1) no SUGGESTION: line present, (2) multiple SUGGESTION: lines (only first captured), (3) SUGGESTION: with empty value, (4) SUGGESTION: with malformed/missing colon. Define 'malformed sentinel' precisely (e.g., 'SUGGESTION:' with no trailing value after trimming whitespace').

findings.location: Plan body vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.finding:
1. The plan body mentions a specific implementation location choice: 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — this parenthetical 'or' is a dangling design decision with no corresponding AC that pins the chosen file. No AC says 'Hook is placed in post-bash.sh' or 'Hook is placed in post-all.sh', so there is an orphan design choice with no verifiable criterion.
2. The plan body references the 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern' from the consolidated dispatcher architecture, but no AC verifies that this pattern is respected.
3. The term 'Agent tool returns' appears in both the title and body, but the ACs refer to 'Agent tool return' (singular) — this inconsistency is minor but worth aligning.
4. No AC maps directly to 'only first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return' behavior (the 'first-only' constraint is stated in the description but absent from the ACs).
findings.suggested_edit: Add ACs: (a) 'Hook is implemented in [chosen file] — resolve the post-bash.sh vs post-all.sh ambiguity'; (b) 'Only the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per tool return is recorded; subsequent ones are ignored'; (c) 'Hook respects the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN dispatcher pattern'.

findings.location: Plan body: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding:
The plan defers a real design decision to the executor: which file to use ('post-bash.sh OR post-all.sh'). This is not a trivial stylistic choice — the two files likely have different trigger scopes (bash-only vs. all tools), and the correct choice affects whether non-bash Agent tool returns are handled. An agent would need to stop and infer which is correct from the broader codebase context, risking the wrong choice.

Additionally, 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return' does not define the exact sentinel format (e.g., is it 'SUGGESTION: <text>' anywhere in stdout? Must it be on its own line? Is it case-sensitive?). An agent would have to guess.
findings.suggested_edit: Resolve the file choice explicitly before the agent executes. Define the sentinel format precisely, e.g.: 'A line matching the regex ^SUGGESTION: .+$ (case-sensitive, anywhere in the tool output)'.

findings.location: AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' and 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes'
findings.finding: The primary verifiability signal ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') is clear. However, the end-state gap exists between description and ACs: the description states suggestion-record.sh must be called, but no AC verifies *how* it is called (e.g., with what arguments, in what format). If suggestion-record.sh expects a specific argument shape and the hook passes the wrong one, tests might not catch it unless tests explicitly assert argument passing. 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' is verifiable but depends on knowing what 'registered' means in context (a source line? an entry in a config file?), which is not defined.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC that specifies the expected invocation: 'suggestion-record.sh is called with the extracted sentinel value as [argument position/flag], verifiable via test mock/stub'. Clarify what 'registered in consolidated dispatcher' means concretely (e.g., 'a source or case-entry exists in post-all.sh for the Agent tool type').

findings.location: Context and Summary sections
Title vs. Summary and Acceptance Criteria
findings.finding: The plan body and ACs faithfully serve the title. All described work (PostToolUse hook, sentinel extraction, suggestion-record.sh integration, dispatcher registration, test coverage) is consistent with and bounded by the title goal. No scope drift or contradiction detected.

findings.location: Summary + Acceptance Criteria
Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The parenthetical 'or post-all.sh' indicates uncertainty about which dispatcher file to target. If a consolidated dispatcher already handles all tool types, adding a separate post-bash.sh solely for this one hook (one call-site) would introduce a new file/layer used at exactly one location — a potential Rule-of-Three violation. Cannot confirm without seeing the live dispatcher architecture, but the ambiguity itself is a plan smell.
findings.suggested_edit: Before implementation, explicitly confirm via the codebase whether a consolidated dispatcher (post-all.sh or equivalent) already exists and handles Agent tool returns. Commit to one target file in the plan rather than leaving it open with 'or'.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The AC mentions edge-case coverage but does not enumerate which edge cases must be covered. Missing explicit coverage requirements for: (1) no SUGGESTION: line present (should be a no-op), (2) multiple SUGGESTION: lines (only first extracted), (3) malformed sentinel — what 'malformed' means is undefined (empty value? missing colon? extra colons?), (4) SUGGESTION: in the middle vs. end of output, (5) empty/null Agent tool return. Without explicit edge-case enumeration, a minimal implementation that passes a happy-path test only would still satisfy the written AC. The plan is in RED mode, making this gap especially consequential.
findings.suggested_edit: Expand AC to list specific test cases: empty return (no-op), single valid SUGGESTION: (recorded), multiple SUGGESTION: lines (only first recorded), SUGGESTION: with no value after colon (warns, no crash), SUGGESTION: with extra colons in value (accepted as-is or defined behavior), and non-Agent tool return (ignored).

findings.location: Summary: 'calls suggestion-record.sh' and 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)'
findings.finding: The plan assumes: (1) suggestion-record.sh already exists and has a known interface, (2) a 'consolidated dispatcher architecture' with a CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern is already in place and the hook can be registered into it. Both are asserted without citing a file path, function signature, or cross-reference. An executor would need to verify these before writing the hook, since a wrong interface assumption would require rework.
findings.suggested_edit: Add explicit references: e.g., 'suggestion-record.sh is located at plugins/dso/hooks/suggestion-record.sh and accepts arguments X Y Z' and 'the consolidated dispatcher is at plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-all.sh and hooks register via pattern Z'. These can be pulled from the story context (.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4) before writing the plan.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: Only one file is named as a target ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'), but PostToolUse hooks for Agent tool returns typically require registration in a hook manifest or configuration file (e.g., .claude/settings.json or equivalent hook-registry). No such registration file is mentioned. If the dispatcher auto-discovers hooks by convention, that assumption is unverified (see E4). Missing consumers: any test harness or integration test that validates dispatcher routing may also need updating.
findings.suggested_edit: Enumerate all files to be created or modified: (1) the dispatcher/hook implementation file, (2) any hook-registration/manifest file, (3) new test file(s). Confirm whether auto-discovery or explicit registration is used.

findings.location: Summary vs. Acceptance Criteria: 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' vs. 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: There is a mild internal tension: the Summary hedges on the target file ('post-bash.sh or post-all.sh'), while the AC firmly states 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'. If post-bash.sh is chosen, it may itself need to be registered in a consolidated dispatcher — adding an implicit prerequisite step not captured elsewhere. This creates ambiguity about the definition of 'done' for the registration AC. Not a hard contradiction, but the uncertainty in the Summary makes the registration AC unverifiable as written.
findings.suggested_edit: Resolve the file target in the Summary before finalizing the AC, so 'registered in consolidated dispatcher' has a concrete meaning that can be verified.

findings.location: AC: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes'
findings.finding: The primary AC is a test suite run, which is mechanically measurable. However, 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return and calls suggestion-record.sh' and 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' are stated as requirements but no verification method is specified beyond the test suite. If the test suite does not yet contain tests for these behaviours (RED mode — tests are expected to fail), it is unclear whether passing the suite at the end is sufficient evidence, or whether the agent must also write new tests first. The plan says 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' as a separate AC, which implies the agent must write tests, but leaves 'edge cases' undefined (e.g., no SUGGESTION line, multiple SUGGESTION lines, malformed value, empty return). An agent could write a minimal passing test and consider this done.
findings.suggested_edit: Enumerate the required edge-case tests explicitly, e.g.: (a) no SUGGESTION: line → no call to suggestion-record.sh, (b) multiple SUGGESTION: lines → only first is recorded, (c) malformed sentinel (e.g. 'SUGGESTION:' with no value) → warning emitted, no crash, (d) well-formed sentinel → suggestion-record.sh called with correct argument.

findings.location: Plan body vs. AC list
findings.finding:
1. The plan body mentions integration with the 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern' but no AC maps to or tests this integration. The executor has no measurable criterion to verify this integration is correct.
2. The plan body says the hook should go in 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — parenthetical 'or' implies a decision has not been made — but the AC 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' does not specify which file, so it cannot be verified unambiguously.
3. 'suggestion-record.sh' is mentioned in both the description and AC, which is consistent, but neither section defines what a 'well-formed' versus 'malformed' sentinel is, creating a gap between the described work and the verifiable criterion.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC for the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN integration or remove it from the description. Resolve the post-bash.sh vs post-all.sh ambiguity in both the description and the 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' AC. Add a brief inline definition of a well-formed SUGGESTION: sentinel (e.g. 'SUGGESTION: <non-empty-string>').

findings.location: Plan body: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The 'or' between post-bash.sh and post-all.sh is an unresolved design decision left to the executor. An agent must stop and guess which file to modify. Similarly, 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return' does not define the exact regex/format of the sentinel (is it anchored to start-of-line? case-sensitive? what delimiter follows 'SUGGESTION:'?). The phrase 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' references an internal pattern without explaining it, requiring the agent to read external documentation (.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4) to proceed, and that external resource may or may not be accessible to the reviewing agent.
findings.suggested_edit: Specify: (1) the exact target file (post-bash.sh or post-all.sh, not both); (2) the canonical sentinel format, e.g. `^SUGGESTION: .+` anchored at line start, case-sensitive; (3) a brief inline description of the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN integration point so the agent does not need to consult the ticket to understand what to do.

findings.location: AC: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' and 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The end-state is 'tests pass', but the plan is in RED mode, meaning tests are expected to fail at the start. The plan does not describe the sequence: write failing tests → implement → tests go green, so the termination condition (green suite) is inferable but not explicit. There is also no AC covering registration verification — 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' has no automated check described, meaning the agent could implement the hook but forget to register it and still pass the test suite if the tests only call the hook script directly.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a test or shell check that exercises the hook via the dispatcher (not just the script directly) to verify registration. Explicitly state the RED→GREEN flow: 'Write failing tests first, implement hook, confirm tests/run-all.sh passes.'

findings.location: Plan body: Context and Summary sections
Title vs. Summary and AC
findings.finding: The title ('Implement PostToolUse hook to extract SUGGESTION: sentinel from sub-agent returns') is faithfully reflected in the summary and ACs. No out-of-scope work is described, and no promised work is missing from the ACs (modulo the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN gap flagged in E1). No contradiction between headline intent and detailed steps.

findings.location: Summary + Acceptance Criteria
Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The plan hedges between two target files ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)') without resolving which one is correct. If a consolidated dispatcher already exists and handles all tool types, adding a parallel per-tool dispatcher for Agent tool returns could duplicate routing logic (NIH / gratuitous branching). Conversely, if the correct file is already identified, the 'or' ambiguity suggests the author has not verified the existing dispatcher structure. Cannot confirm without the live codebase.
findings.suggested_edit: Before writing any code, confirm which dispatcher file Agent tool PostToolUse events flow through, and remove the ambiguity from the plan (commit to one file).

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The AC mentions edge-case coverage but does not enumerate any specific cases. For a sentinel-extraction function the following are non-obvious boundary cases that should be listed explicitly: (1) return with no SUGGESTION: line, (2) return with multiple SUGGESTION: lines (plan says 'first' — test must assert that only the first is recorded), (3) SUGGESTION: line with a blank/empty body (the 'malformed' path), (4) SUGGESTION: appearing mid-line vs. at line start, (5) Agent tool return that is empty/null. Without naming these, an executor may write only a happy-path test. The testing mode is RED, so failing tests must be written first — but there is no description of what those failing tests assert, making it hard to know when they are correctly red.
findings.suggested_edit: Expand the 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' AC to list at minimum: no SUGGESTION line (no-op), multiple SUGGESTION lines (only first recorded), malformed sentinel body (warn, no crash), empty return, and SUGGESTION not at line-start. Also note what the RED test fixture will assert before the implementation exists.

findings.location: Summary: 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' and 'calls suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding: The plan asserts two things that must be verified against the live codebase before the executor can proceed: (1) that a 'consolidated dispatcher architecture' following the 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern' already exists and that the new hook can simply be registered into it; and (2) that 'suggestion-record.sh' already exists and has a stable interface the hook can call. If either helper is absent or has a different API, the implementation approach changes significantly. The plan provides no instructions for the executor to verify these before starting.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit pre-condition check step: 'Verify post-bash.sh/post-all.sh dispatcher exists and supports hook registration; verify suggestion-record.sh exists and document its expected call signature in this plan.' This protects against the executor discovering mid-implementation that a dependency is missing.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' and 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: The plan names candidate files (post-bash.sh, post-all.sh, suggestion-record.sh) but does not name: (a) any hook-registration manifest or configuration file that would also need updating, (b) any test file(s) that will be created/modified (file paths are absent), or (c) any caller that currently invokes suggestion-record.sh (to validate the calling convention). The 'or' between the two dispatcher files leaves the actual edit target unresolved. Blast radius is not acknowledged — if Agent tool returns currently flow through multiple dispatchers, more than one file may need updating.
findings.suggested_edit: Pin the exact dispatcher file, list the test file path(s) that will be created, name any manifest/config that registers hooks, and note whether other dispatchers must be checked for Agent tool routing.

findings.location: Testing Mode: 'RED' vs. Acceptance Criteria: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes'
findings.finding: The testing mode is declared RED (write failing tests first), but the first acceptance criterion is 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes', which is a GREEN criterion. This creates a logical contradiction: under a strict RED-first workflow the suite should be failing at the end of the RED step, yet the AC says it must pass. Additionally, the AC 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' is not paired with any test or verification step, making it unverifiable independently of the full test run.
findings.suggested_edit: Split the AC into two phases: (1) RED phase — 'tests/run-all.sh has new failing tests covering the sentinel extraction cases'; (2) GREEN phase — 'tests/run-all.sh passes after implementation.' Alternatively, clarify that the 'passes' AC applies only after the GREEN implementation step.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes'
findings.finding: The primary measurability criterion ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') is a runnable command, which is good. However, it depends on the test suite actually covering the new behavior — if no new tests are added or existing tests don't exercise the new hook, the suite could pass trivially without validating the feature. The AC 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' is stated but gives no concrete list of which edge cases must pass, making it difficult to confirm completeness without codebase access. Additionally, the description references reading a story file via '.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4', which is an out-of-session external dependency the agent must resolve before work can begin.
findings.suggested_edit: Enumerate the specific edge cases that tests must cover (e.g., no SUGGESTION: line, multiple SUGGESTION: lines, malformed sentinel format, empty return, non-Agent tool return) directly in the AC so measurability is self-contained.

findings.location: Summary section and Context section
findings.finding: The plan describes mechanism clearly (extract SUGGESTION: sentinels, record via suggestion-record.sh, warn on malformed) but only lightly implies the beneficiary/problem. The story reference ('story 95f8-7ec4') is where the full user/problem context lives, but it is external and not summarized in the plan itself. An agent or reviewer cannot determine WHO benefits (e.g., orchestrating agents, operators, automated pipelines) or WHAT failure/gap this fixes without resolving that external reference. A minimal 'problem statement' sentence is missing from the plan body.
findings.suggested_edit: Add one sentence in the Summary or Context explaining the problem: e.g., 'Sub-agent suggestions are currently lost because no hook captures them; this causes orchestrators to miss actionable recommendations surfaced by sub-agents.'

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'; Testing Mode: RED
findings.finding: The plan declares Testing Mode RED (write failing tests first) and lists 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' as an AC, but provides no enumeration of which edge cases tests must cover, no specification of the test file or framework to use, and no description of what a RED test looks like for this hook (e.g., expected exit code, expected output, mock inputs). Without this, an agent cannot write the correct failing tests before implementing the hook. Failure-path coverage (malformed sentinel, multiple sentinels, non-Agent tool returns) is mentioned in the description but not translated into concrete test scenarios.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit test plan subsection listing: (1) Happy path: single well-formed SUGGESTION: line extracted and passed to suggestion-record.sh; (2) Multiple SUGGESTION: lines — only first is recorded; (3) Malformed sentinel — warning emitted, no crash, no record written; (4) No SUGGESTION: line — no-op; (5) Non-Agent tool return — hook skips gracefully. Specify that tests live in tests/ and use the project's existing shell-test harness.

findings.location: Summary vs. Acceptance Criteria
findings.finding: Two coherence gaps exist: (1) The Summary mentions 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern' as part of the consolidated dispatcher architecture integration, but this terminology appears nowhere in the Acceptance Criteria — there is no AC verifying that the hook correctly participates in the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern. (2) The Summary says the hook goes in 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — the ambiguous 'or' is never resolved in the ACs, leaving the target file undefined. No AC specifies which file the hook must be registered in, making 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' unverifiable as written.
findings.suggested_edit: Resolve the 'post-bash.sh or post-all.sh' ambiguity: pick one and name it in both the description and the AC. Add an AC or sub-bullet for CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern compliance if it is a required contract, or remove the mention from the Summary if it is not tested.

findings.location: AC: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' and 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The primary completability criterion ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') is a full suite run rather than a targeted verification, which is fine IF the suite is fast and self-contained. However, it is unclear whether 'tests/run-all.sh' exists and runs without external dependencies (e.g., requires a live DSO service, network calls to '.claude/scripts/dso ticket show'). The instruction to read story context via 'dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4' in the plan body also implies an external live system. If that script or the test suite requires out-of-session infrastructure, the criterion is not completable within one session. The edge-case test criterion ('Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases') is measurable in principle but no specific cases are enumerated, making it hard for an agent to know when done.
findings.suggested_edit: 1) Explicitly state that tests/run-all.sh is self-contained and offline-runnable. 2) Enumerate the required edge cases (e.g., no SUGGESTION line, multiple SUGGESTION lines, malformed sentinel, empty return, non-Agent tool call) so the criterion is unambiguously satisfiable.

findings.location: Summary section
AC: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'; Summary section; Testing Mode: RED
findings.finding: The plan correctly sets Testing Mode to RED (write failing tests first), which is good. However, the testing plan is very thin: it names no specific test file, no test framework/runner details, no specific edge cases that must be covered (only the vague phrase 'edge cases'), and no failure-path coverage is described (e.g., what happens when suggestion-record.sh itself fails, or when the tool return is from a non-Agent tool). The malformed-sentinel warning behavior is listed as an AC but has no corresponding test described. Without knowing which test file/framework is used, it is ambiguous whether behavioral (not just structural) tests will be written.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Tests' sub-section that names: (a) the test file (e.g., tests/hooks/test-post-agent-suggestion.sh), (b) the test framework (bats, shellspec, etc.), (c) explicit cases: happy path (one SUGGESTION line extracted), multiple SUGGESTION lines (only first taken), no SUGGESTION line (no call to suggestion-record.sh), malformed sentinel (warning emitted, no crash/exit 1), non-Agent tool call (hook is a no-op), suggestion-record.sh unavailable (graceful warning). Tie each case to an AC bullet.

findings.location: Summary vs. AC; AC bullet 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding:
Several coherence and coverage gaps exist:
1. The Summary mentions 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — the ambiguity ('or') is carried forward to the AC 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' without resolving which file. The AC is therefore ambiguous about what constitutes done, and the description leaves a decision unresolved that should be made at plan time.
2. The AC bullet 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' has no corresponding description of what constitutes a 'malformed sentinel' — the term is used without definition, creating a terminology gap.
3. The plan describes the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern in the Summary but this pattern is never referenced in any AC, making it an orphan-described element with no verifiable criterion.
4. The story reference ('95f8-7ec4', '.claude/scripts/dso ticket show') is in the description but the full context is gated behind an external command; any criteria derived from that full story context are invisible to the reviewer/agent unless that command is run, creating a potential orphan-criteria risk.
findings.suggested_edit: 1) Resolve 'post-bash.sh OR post-all.sh' to a single concrete file in both description and AC. 2) Define 'malformed sentinel' (e.g., 'SUGGESTION: line missing required fields X and Y'). 3) Either add an AC for CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN integration or remove it from the description. 4) Inline the essential context from the story rather than relying on an external live command.

findings.location: AC: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' and 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: Most criteria are mechanically verifiable (run a shell script, check output), which is good. However, two sub-issues reduce full confidence: (1) 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' is a global test-suite gate — if unrelated pre-existing failures exist, this criterion may fail for reasons outside the agent's control, making it not purely measurable against this ticket's changes. (2) 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' is verifiable by inspection, but 'consolidated dispatcher architecture' and the exact registration mechanism are not defined in the plan, so an agent cannot confirm this criterion is met without access to the existing codebase. These are not blockers but introduce ambiguity.
findings.suggested_edit: Narrow the test-suite criterion to the tests added/modified by this ticket (e.g., 'tests specifically covering the PostToolUse hook pass'). Briefly describe what 'registered in consolidated dispatcher' means concretely (e.g., 'hook entry appears in post-all.sh dispatch table').

findings.location: Summary section: 'Implement story 95f8-7ec4: PostToolUse hook on Agent tool returns that extracts SUGGESTION: sentinels.'
findings.finding: The plan names the technical mechanism clearly but does not explicitly state WHO benefits or WHAT problem is being fixed. The beneficiary and problem are implicit: presumably sub-agents emit SUGGESTION: sentinels that are currently lost because no hook captures them. The plan relies entirely on an external story reference ('.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4') for context, which is not accessible in this review. A reviewer or agent without that story context cannot confirm the problem statement or the user/caller.
findings.suggested_edit: Add one sentence to the Summary, e.g.: 'Currently, SUGGESTION: sentinels emitted by sub-agents in Agent tool returns are silently dropped. This hook captures and records them so that callers/orchestrators can act on sub-agent suggestions.'

findings.location: AC: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The plan states 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' but does not enumerate which edge cases must be covered. No edge cases are identified anywhere in the plan body. Key boundary/failure cases that are likely critical include: (1) no SUGGESTION: line present (no-op expected), (2) multiple SUGGESTION: lines (only first extracted — per the description), (3) malformed sentinel (warn, no crash — mentioned in AC but not tied to a specific test scenario), (4) empty/whitespace Agent tool return, (5) SUGGESTION: at different positions (first line, last line, mid-output). Without enumeration, 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' is unverifiable and risks being treated as a change-detector rather than a behavioral test.
findings.suggested_edit: Expand the testing AC to enumerate required cases, e.g.: 'Tests must cover: (a) output with no SUGGESTION: line → no call to suggestion-record.sh; (b) output with exactly one SUGGESTION: line → suggestion-record.sh called once; (c) output with multiple SUGGESTION: lines → only first is recorded; (d) malformed SUGGESTION: line (e.g. missing value) → warning emitted to stderr, no crash, no call to suggestion-record.sh; (e) empty tool return → no-op.'

findings.location: Summary: 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; AC: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: Two coherence/terminology issues: (1) The Summary wavers on the target file ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'), introducing ambiguity about which file will be modified. The AC says 'registered in consolidated dispatcher' without resolving this. An agent must make an undocumented choice. (2) The AC 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' maps to the description's 'Warn on malformed sentinel' — consistent — but neither the description nor the ACs clarify what 'malformed' means (wrong format? Missing value after colon? Partial match?). This creates a gap: the described behavior has no precise testable definition. No duplicate criteria found.
findings.suggested_edit: (1) Commit to one target file in both the Summary and AC (e.g., 'post-all.sh'). (2) Define 'malformed sentinel' explicitly, e.g., 'a line starting with SUGGESTION: but with no non-whitespace value following the colon'.

findings.location: Summary + Acceptance Criteria
Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The plan is ambiguous about which dispatcher file to edit ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'), suggesting the author hasn't confirmed which dispatcher already handles Agent tool returns. If a generic 'post-all.sh' dispatcher already routes all tool calls through a common path, adding Agent-specific logic there could be over-broad (affecting non-Agent tools unnecessarily). Conversely, if 'post-bash.sh' is the right target it may not fire on Agent tool returns at all. This 'or' needs resolving against the live codebase before implementation to avoid either NIH duplication (adding a new dispatcher instead of reusing an existing one) or a misplaced hook.
findings.suggested_edit: Before writing code, run `grep -r 'Agent\|PostToolUse' plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/` to identify which dispatcher already receives Agent tool-return events, then remove the '(or post-all.sh)' ambiguity from the plan.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The AC lists 'sentinel extraction edge cases' as a test target but does not enumerate any specific cases. For a sentinel-extraction hook, the following edge cases are contractually important and absent from the plan: (1) Agent return with no SUGGESTION: line at all (should be a no-op, not a warning), (2) Agent return with multiple SUGGESTION: lines (plan says 'first' is extracted — what happens to the rest?), (3) malformed sentinel (e.g. 'SUGGESTION:' with no trailing text — AC says 'warn', but no test spec is given), (4) SUGGESTION: embedded mid-line vs. at line start, (5) empty/null Agent tool return. Without specifying these cases the test plan is thin and an executor may write only happy-path tests while the AC checkbox still gets checked.
findings.suggested_edit: Expand the test AC to explicitly list: (a) no SUGGESTION present → no call to suggestion-record.sh; (b) multiple SUGGESTION: lines → only first is recorded; (c) malformed sentinel ('SUGGESTION:' with no body) → warning emitted, no crash, suggestion-record.sh not called; (d) SUGGESTION: not at line start → behaviour defined (ignore or extract?); (e) empty tool return → no-op.

findings.location: Summary: 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' and 'calls suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding: Two unverified premises underpin the implementation: (1) 'suggestion-record.sh' is asserted to already exist and have a known calling convention, but this is never confirmed. If it doesn't exist or has a different interface, the hook will silently fail or error. (2) The 'consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)' is referenced as an existing contract the new hook must honour, but no description is given of what that pattern requires (e.g. specific exit codes, environment variables, output format). An executor cannot implement the hook correctly without knowing these contracts.
findings.suggested_edit: Add to the plan: (a) confirm suggestion-record.sh path and calling convention (e.g. `suggestion-record.sh "<text>"`) with a pointer to its source; (b) summarise the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN dispatcher contract (required env vars, expected exit codes, stdout/stderr conventions) so the hook can be wired in correctly.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' and 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: The plan identifies only one primary edit target (the dispatcher file) plus a registration step, but doesn't acknowledge: (1) any hook-registration file or manifest that lists active hooks (if hooks must be declared somewhere for the dispatcher to invoke them, that file is a required edit target); (2) whether the dispatcher expects hooks to live in a specific subdirectory or follow a naming convention; (3) whether any test-infrastructure files (e.g. a test fixtures directory or a mock for suggestion-record.sh) need to be created. The 'or' in the target file name also signals that the blast radius hasn't been fully scoped.
findings.suggested_edit: Before finalising the plan, audit: `grep -r 'post-bash\|post-all\|PostToolUse' plugins/dso/hooks/` to find all registration points, then list each file that must be edited alongside the dispatcher itself.

findings.location: Testing Mode: 'RED' vs. Acceptance Criteria: 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes'
findings.finding: The plan declares Testing Mode RED (write failing tests first, then make them pass), but the first AC checkpoint is 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes'. In a strict RED-before-GREEN workflow the suite should be FAILING when the tests are first written; requiring the suite to pass as AC-1 conflates the RED phase exit condition with the GREEN phase exit condition. This creates ambiguity for an executor about when to check AC-1 (only after full implementation, or also after writing tests). Additionally, the plan says 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' in the summary, but the AC does not include a corresponding checkbox verifying the warning behaviour, creating a minor gap between the narrative and the verifiable criteria.
findings.suggested_edit: Reorder AC so that AC-1 ('tests/run-all.sh passes') is the final checkpoint, and add an intermediate RED-phase checkpoint such as 'New tests written and failing before implementation begins'. Also add an AC item for 'Malformed sentinel emits a warning to stderr and does not cause a non-zero exit'.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The plan presents a genuine unresolved architectural decision — 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' — with an explicit 'or' that the executor must resolve. This is not a minor wording issue: the choice determines which dispatcher file is modified, which tool types the hook fires on, and whether the hook is scoped only to bash tool returns or all tool returns. The phrase 'figure out' is implicitly pushed onto the agent. Similarly, 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return' does not specify the exact regex/format that constitutes a well-formed vs. malformed sentinel, leaving the executor to invent the contract. The AC says 'Warns on malformed sentinel' but never defines what 'malformed' means (e.g., missing space after colon, empty value, multiple lines, non-UTF8).
findings.suggested_edit: 1) Decide and hard-code the dispatcher file: e.g., 'Add the hook to post-all.sh so it fires for all tool types, filtering on tool_name == Agent.' 2) Define the sentinel format explicitly: e.g., 'A well-formed sentinel is a line matching ^SUGGESTION: .+ (non-empty value after the colon-space). Any line starting with SUGGESTION: that does not match this pattern is malformed.'

findings.location: Title vs. Summary vs. Acceptance Criteria

Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)'; 'records it via suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding: The plan makes three unverified structural assertions that an executor must confirm against the live codebase before acting: (1) that a 'consolidated dispatcher architecture' with a CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern already exists at the named paths; (2) that post-bash.sh and/or post-all.sh exist and are the correct extension points for a PostToolUse hook; (3) that suggestion-record.sh exists, is on PATH, and has a stable interface the hook can call. If any of these are absent, the executor will stall mid-implementation. The story context reference ('.claude/scripts/dso ticket show 95f8-7ec4') is an external runtime command, not inline content, so the reviewer cannot confirm these assumptions.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Prerequisites / verified facts' section that explicitly states: the exact relative paths of the dispatcher files, the calling convention of suggestion-record.sh (arguments, exit codes), and a pointer to where the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN registration pattern is documented or exemplified, so the executor has a concrete code reference rather than an asserted claim.

findings.location: Summary: 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return, and records it via suggestion-record.sh'
findings.finding: The plan implies introducing a new PostToolUse hook specifically for sentinel extraction. It is unclear whether an existing hook or utility already handles sentinel scanning in the same dispatcher pipeline (e.g., a generic output-scanner hook). If such a facility exists, adding a dedicated hook would duplicate logic at a single call-site and violate the Rule-of-Three. This cannot be confirmed without inspecting the live codebase.
findings.suggested_edit: Before writing a new hook, verify that no existing post-tool scanner already processes SUGGESTION: lines. If one exists, extend it rather than adding a parallel hook.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The target file for implementing the hook is expressed as an 'or' choice ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)') without a decision. The executor must decide which dispatcher to use, and the choice has architectural implications — a wrong decision (e.g. putting it in post-bash.sh when Agent tool returns route through post-all.sh) would produce a silently non-functional hook. This is an explicit decision-deferral pushed onto the executor.
findings.suggested_edit: Commit to a single target file in the plan. If the intent is to catch Agent tool returns specifically (not just Bash tool returns), name post-all.sh (or whatever dispatcher handles Agent tool invocations) and remove the ambiguity. Alternatively, add an AC that the executor first reads the existing dispatcher routing logic and documents which file handles 'Agent' tool type before implementing.

findings.location: Title vs. Summary: 'PostToolUse hook … extracts SUGGESTION: sentinel from sub-agent returns' vs. 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)'
findings.finding: The title and AC focus entirely on extraction and recording of SUGGESTION: sentinels. The summary additionally mentions 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern' without explaining what this is or how it interacts with the sentinel extraction. It is unclear whether the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern is a pre-existing integration requirement the executor must respect, a hint about sentinel format, or out-of-scope context. If it implies the hook must also handle CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN signals, the ACs are incomplete. If it is just background context, it should be stated as such.
findings.suggested_edit: Either remove the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN reference from the summary if it is pure context, or add an explicit AC that describes how the hook interacts with the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern (e.g., 'SUGGESTION: sentinels are only recorded when the confidence marker is X').

findings.location: Summary and AC: 'calls suggestion-record.sh', 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher', 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The plan makes several unverified structural claims about the codebase that the executor must confirm before acting: (1) 'suggestion-record.sh' exists and accepts the expected argument format; (2) a 'consolidated dispatcher architecture' exists at the named paths; (3) the named dispatcher files (post-bash.sh / post-all.sh) are the correct integration points for PostToolUse on Agent tool returns. If any of these don't exist or have a different interface/location, the plan cannot be executed as written.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a short 'Pre-conditions' or 'Verify first' section listing: (a) path and interface of suggestion-record.sh, (b) path of the consolidated dispatcher and the registration mechanism, (c) which dispatcher file handles Agent (not Bash) tool returns. This converts implicit assumptions into explicit checkpoints.

findings.location: Summary: 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return'
findings.finding: It is unclear whether sentinel-extraction logic already exists somewhere in the dispatcher or hook ecosystem (e.g., a shared parse utility) that could be reused, or whether the plan is calling for new bespoke extraction code. If a generic line-extraction helper already exists, writing a new one-off extractor would be NIH. Without access to the codebase this cannot be confirmed, but the plan does not acknowledge the possibility.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a note to the plan: 'Check existing hooks/utilities for reusable sentinel-parsing helpers before writing new extraction logic.' This is a lightweight guard against NIH without requiring codebase access now.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: The target file for the hook is left as an open choice ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'). The executor must pick one without guidance on the deciding factors (e.g., whether 'Agent tool' returns are classified under bash or all). 'CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern' is mentioned but not defined in the plan — an executor unfamiliar with the codebase cannot know what this pattern requires without reading external documents. Additionally, 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return' does not specify what constitutes a 'malformed' sentinel (wrong prefix capitalisation? missing value after colon? extra whitespace?), leaving validation logic undefined.

Define 'malformed sentinel' explicitly (e.g., 'a line starting with SUGGESTION: but containing no non-whitespace content after the colon'). 3. findings.location: Title vs. Summary/ACs

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five bullet points
findings.finding: The verification story is incomplete. AC1 ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') is a generic regression gate, not evidence that the new behaviour works. AC2–4 describe outputs but give no verification step for each: there is no stated command, fixture, or observable artefact that confirms (a) the hook fires on an actual Agent tool return event, (b) suggestion-record.sh is called with the correct argument, or (c) the malformed-sentinel warning reaches the right stream. The plan also omits what 'done' looks like for the consolidated-dispatcher registration (e.g. a grep/diff of the dispatcher file). Without these, the agent has no reliable stopping signal beyond 'tests pass', which may not exercise the new code path at all.
findings.suggested_edit: Add explicit per-AC verification commands, e.g.: AC2: 'Run the new unit test with a fixture Agent-tool-return payload and assert suggestion-record.sh is called with the extracted value.' AC3: 'Run with a malformed sentinel fixture and assert a warning line appears on stderr and exit code is 0.' AC4: 'grep -n post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh) for the registration entry and confirm it is present.' AC5: list the edge-case fixtures by name (empty body, multiple SUGGESTION: lines, sentinel mid-line, etc.).

findings.location: Summary and Acceptance Criteria
Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'
findings.finding: Two mutually exclusive target files are named ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)') with no resolution criteria. If the agent picks the wrong one it may silently fail to intercept Agent tool returns (which may not be Bash tool events). Additionally, the plan mentions suggestion-record.sh as a callee but does not confirm it exists, has a stable interface, or needs updating. The dispatcher registration mechanism ('CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern') is referenced but not described, so the agent cannot verify the registration is correct. Finally, the full set of files that must change (dispatcher map, the new hook file, any test fixtures, any manifest/loader) is not enumerated.
findings.suggested_edit: Resolve 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' to a single definitive file with justification (e.g. 'Agent tool returns emit a PostToolUse event on the all-tools dispatcher, therefore post-all.sh'). Explicitly list every file to be created or modified: the hook implementation file, the dispatcher registration line, the test file(s), and any manifest. Confirm suggestion-record.sh's expected call signature in the plan.

findings.location: Testing Mode: RED; Summary: '...post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; AC4: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'
findings.finding: Minor internal tensions: (1) Testing mode is declared RED (write failing tests first), but the plan describes implementing the hook and registering it, with tests as an AC rather than a prerequisite — this could lead the agent to write implementation before tests, undermining TDD intent. (2) The 'or' ambiguity in the target file (post-bash.sh vs post-all.sh) means the registration location (AC4) is also unresolved, creating a contradiction between the summary and AC4 since they both reference the dispatcher but with different possible targets. These are not hard contradictions but they are coherence gaps that could cause confusion during execution.
findings.suggested_edit: Reorder the ACs so the failing test (RED) is the first deliverable, before implementation ACs. Resolve the file ambiguity in the summary so AC4 references a specific file name.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five bullet points
findings.finding: The plan's acceptance criteria mix a generic test-suite pass ('bash tests/run-all.sh passes') with four behavioural assertions, but provides no specification of what 'done' looks like for the behavioural assertions: (1) 'first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return' — no definition of what constitutes a valid vs. malformed sentinel format, so an implementer cannot know when the extraction logic is complete; (2) 'Warns on malformed sentinel (no crash)' — no description of what warning mechanism is used (stderr, a log file, a specific exit code) or what malformed means; (3) 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' — no statement of which dispatcher file is the canonical registration point (the summary waffles between post-bash.sh and post-all.sh); (4) 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' — no enumeration of which edge cases are required, leaving the stop condition undefined. Without these, an agent cannot determine when to stop iterating.
findings.suggested_edit: Expand each AC bullet to include: (a) the exact SUGGESTION: sentinel format (e.g., 'SUGGESTION: <non-empty text on same line'); (b) the definition of 'malformed' (e.g., 'SUGGESTION:' with no trailing text, or multiple SUGGESTION: lines); (c) the exact warning channel (e.g., 'prints to stderr, exits 0'); (d) the authoritative dispatcher file; (e) an explicit list of required edge-case test scenarios (empty output, no sentinel, one sentinel, multiple sentinels, malformed sentinel).

findings.location: Summary + Acceptance Criteria
Summary: 'plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' and 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture'
findings.finding: Two separate issues: (1) The plan names two candidate files ('post-bash.sh or post-all.sh') without resolving which one is the actual target. This ambiguity means the agent must guess, risking implementation in the wrong file or both, and leaves the blast-radius unacknowledged for whichever file is NOT chosen. (2) The plan references 'suggestion-record.sh' as the recording mechanism but does not confirm this script already exists, nor does it name its expected interface (arguments, exit codes). If it does not exist or has a different signature, the hook will silently fail or error. Both require codebase confirmation.
findings.suggested_edit: (1) Resolve the dispatcher ambiguity before implementation — pick exactly one file and state why. (2) Add a note confirming suggestion-record.sh exists and documenting its calling convention (e.g., 'suggestion-record.sh "<text>"'), or add an AC for creating it if it does not exist.

findings.location: Summary ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)') vs. AC ('Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher') vs. Testing Mode ('RED')
findings.finding: Two internal contradictions: (1) The summary presents two mutually exclusive target files ('post-bash.sh or post-all.sh') without resolution, while the AC states only 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' — the AC cannot be verified if the summary does not commit to a single file. (2) Testing Mode is 'RED', implying tests are written first and must fail before implementation, but the AC bullet 'bash tests/run-all.sh passes' describes a green state. These are not contradictory in TDD if interpreted as 'RED phase now → GREEN is the end-state', but the AC is written entirely in green-pass language with no mention of the RED-phase deliverable (failing tests), creating ambiguity about what the agent should produce in this session vs. a later session.
findings.suggested_edit: (1) Choose one dispatcher file. (2) Either split the ACs into RED-phase deliverables ('failing tests that specify behaviour') and GREEN-phase deliverables ('hook implementation + tests pass'), or clarify that this ticket covers both phases end-to-end.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five bullet points
findings.finding: The primary verification gate is `bash tests/run-all.sh passes`, which is a good binary signal, but the plan does not specify what 'done' looks like for the intermediate steps. Specifically: (1) The plan says to add a PostToolUse hook to 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' without committing to which file, leaving the end-state ambiguous. (2) The AC 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' is stated without describing what registration looks like (a config entry, a source line, a function call, etc.), so it is not independently verifiable. (3) 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' does not enumerate the edge cases, making it impossible to know when test coverage is sufficient. Without these specifics, an agent could declare done with partial coverage.
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Commit to a specific file (post-bash.sh OR post-all.sh, not both). 2. Add an AC that names the dispatcher registration mechanism (e.g., 'A CLAUDE_TOOL_USE_POST entry in .claude/settings.json points to the hook'). 3. List required edge cases explicitly: empty return, multiple SUGGESTION: lines, malformed sentinel (missing text after colon), no sentinel present.

findings.location: Summary ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)') vs. AC ('Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher') vs. Testing Mode ('RED')
findings.finding: Two internal contradictions: (1) The Summary is ambiguous about the target file ('post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'), while the AC requires registration in a 'consolidated dispatcher' — these are potentially different concepts (one is a file, the other is a registration mechanism), and the plan never resolves which file implements the hook versus where it is registered, creating a logical gap. findings.location: Context / Summary sections

findings.location: Title and Summary
Acceptance Criteria: 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The testing plan is in RED mode, which is correct. However, 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases' names no specific edge cases and provides no guidance on what scenarios the tests must cover. Meaningful RED-phase tests require explicit contract scenarios. Missing cases include: (a) Agent return with zero SUGGESTION: lines, (b) return with multiple SUGGESTION: lines (first-only extraction), (c) malformed sentinel format (warn path), (d) empty/whitespace return, (e) suggestion-record.sh receiving correct arguments. Without specifying these, an executor could write trivially-passing tests that miss the essential contract.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)'; 'calls suggestion-record.sh'

This is a real design decision deferred to the executor. Additionally, 'extracts the first SUGGESTION: sentinel per return' does not specify the expected format or regex pattern, and 'Warns on malformed sentinel' does not define what constitutes 'malformed' (e.g., missing message after colon? wrong capitalisation? duplicate sentinels?). The executor would need to stop and ask before choosing between the two files, and before deciding what a valid vs. malformed sentinel looks like.
findings.suggested_edit: Resolve the dispatcher file choice in the plan (e.g., 'Add hook to post-all.sh because it covers all tool types' or vice versa). Document the exact sentinel format — e.g., 'SUGGESTION: <non-empty message on a single line' — and the malformed definition, e.g., 'SUGGESTION: with empty/missing body, or appearing more than once'.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher' and 'Tests cover sentinel extraction edge cases'
findings.finding: The acceptance criteria name the desired end-states but do not describe HOW to reach them. There is no description of what 'registered in consolidated dispatcher' concretely means (e.g., what line/block to add, what function to call). 'Edge cases' for sentinel extraction are not enumerated anywhere, so the executor cannot know when test coverage is sufficient. The summary's implementation steps reduce to a single vague bullet; there is no ordered sequence of steps from current state to done-state, leaving a gap between the described work and the verification criteria.
findings.suggested_edit: Add explicit implementation steps: (1) which file to edit and what code block to add, (2) how to register the hook (e.g., a specific dispatch table entry), (3) a minimum list of edge cases to test (e.g., no sentinel present, exactly one sentinel, multiple sentinels, sentinel with empty body, sentinel mid-output vs. last line).

findings.location: Summary: 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; Acceptance Criteria: 'Hook registered in consolidated dispatcher'

Without codebase access, it cannot be confirmed that these files exist or that no other dispatcher files are affected.

findings.location: Summary vs. Acceptance Criteria: 'PostToolUse hook' / 'post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)' vs. 'Hook extracts first SUGGESTION: line per Agent tool return'
findings.finding: The Summary frames this as a PostToolUse hook specifically for 'Agent tool returns', implying it should only fire for Agent-type tool invocations. However, the candidate file 'post-bash.sh' suggests a Bash-tool dispatcher, which would fire on Bash tool returns rather than Agent tool returns. This is an internal contradiction: if the intent is to intercept Agent tool returns, post-bash.sh is the wrong target; if post-all.sh is used, the hook must filter to Agent tool type explicitly. The plan does not resolve this tension.

(e.g. multiple SUGGESTION: lines, malformed/partial sentinel, no SUGGESTION: present, empty return). Naming them explicitly would prevent ambiguity.

findings.location: Summary: 'Add a PostToolUse hook to plugins/dso/hooks/dispatchers/post-bash.sh (or post-all.sh)'; 'Integrates with consolidated dispatcher architecture (CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN pattern)'; 'calls suggestion-record.sh'

findings.location: DD1/DD2 and 'Insertion Point in main()' section

New Named Tuple section: '_SchemaValidationResult(NamedTuple)'; 'New Function _validate_findings_schema()' section
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new _SchemaValidationResult NamedTuple used at exactly one call-site (the single insertion point in main()). Under the Rule-of-Three, a plain tuple or a simple dict return would serve the same purpose without a new named abstraction. The NamedTuple is not wrong — it adds readability and type-safety — but whether it clears the 'only one call-site' threshold for abstraction depends on whether S-B (and possibly S-C) will also consume _SchemaValidationResult directly. The plan notes S-B 'will intercept' the result, implying at least a second consumer, but that consumer is out-of-scope here and does not yet exist. If S-B ends up re-using _SchemaValidationResult without modification, the abstraction is justified. If S-B introduces its own wrapper, the NamedTuple becomes a one-shot helper. Cannot confirm without seeing the S-B plan.
findings.suggested_edit: Consider adding a brief comment alongside the NamedTuple declaration stating it is a shared type consumed by both S-A (validator_error / pass paths) and S-B (schema_fail path), so the multi-consumer intent is explicit and the abstraction is self-justifying at review time.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'All T1 tests now pass (GREEN after implementation)' and 'Unit tests pass (exit 0)'
findings.finding: Several acceptance criteria depend on pre-existing T1 test files (tests/skills/dso_ci_review/test_runner_smoke.py with tests named test_validate_findings_schema, test_main_validator_error, test_main_schema_fail) that are not created or modified by this ticket. Similarly, the 'validator_error path emits CRITICAL' AC uses a partial mock script that ends with `pass` and a benign print — it does not actually exercise the code path it claims to verify, making the criterion trivially passable without the real behaviour being correct.

findings.finding: 1. findings.location: Implementation Notes → Subprocess invocation IMPORTANT note: 'the sub-agent MUST read plugins/dso/scripts/write-reviewer-findings.sh and plugins/dso/scripts/validate-review-output.sh to confirm: (a) exact args order … (b) stdin vs file input … (c) any required env vars'
findings.finding: The plan explicitly defers a load-bearing design decision to the executing agent: it provides a 'best-guess' invocation but then says the agent must read two shell scripts and potentially change the invocation. If the agent discovers a different args order, stdin requirement, or required env var, it must deviate from the plan's stated subprocess call with no guidance on what to do when findings conflict. This is an explicit decision-deferral that pushes a real design question onto the executor. Additionally, the Pattern Reference quotes write-reviewer-findings.sh line 150-159 showing the invocation as `validate-review-output.sh code-review-dispatch "$PENDING_FILE" >&2` (stdout redirected to stderr) but the plan's subprocess.run uses capture_output=True — if the validator writes its schema-error output to stdout rather than stderr, the error-parsing logic (which reads stderr) would silently return empty errors on schema_fail. This is an unresolved ambiguity the agent would have to discover at runtime.
findings.suggested_edit: The plan author should read those two scripts NOW and commit to a definitive invocation (args order, stdin vs file, env vars, stdout vs stderr for schema errors) before the agent executes. Remove the 'MUST read and confirm' instruction and replace it with a resolved specification. Clarify whether schema error lines appear on stdout or stderr from validate-review-output.sh, and adjust the rc==1 parsing branch accordingly.

This means a non-trivial investigation step must happen at execution time, not at planning time. The risk is bounded (two shell scripts, one call-site) but real. findings.location: New Named Tuple — '_SchemaValidationResult(NamedTuple)'; 'New Function _validate_findings_schema()'
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new NamedTuple (_SchemaValidationResult) used at exactly one call-site in main(). A plain tuple, a dataclass, or even three named constants would serve equally well at a single call-site, and the Rule-of-Three flag applies. Whether this is over-engineering depends on whether S-B / S-C stories will also import and pattern-match on _SchemaValidationResult — if they do, the abstraction is justified; if S-B simply checks _schema_result.status inline at the same call-site, the NamedTuple adds a layer for no gain. The plan does mention S-B will 'intercept' the result, which lends some justification, but it is not explicit enough to confirm reuse across stories. Cannot determine without seeing S-B's design.
findings.suggested_edit: If S-B will import and type-annotate against _SchemaValidationResult, keep the NamedTuple and note that explicitly in the plan. If S-B only reads .status at the same call-site, simplify to a plain (status, errors) tuple or an inline conditional to avoid the one-site abstraction.

findings.location: DD1/DD2 section and Implementation Notes
Implementation Notes → 'IMPORTANT: Before finalizing the invocation, the sub-agent MUST read …'
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges it has not yet confirmed the exact subprocess invocation args against the real shell scripts (write-reviewer-findings.sh and validate-review-output.sh) and explicitly defers that audit to the executing agent. This means a key detail in the plan body — the exact argument order and stdin-vs-file contract — is conditionally correct, not validated. If the audit reveals divergence, the invocation shown in the plan will be wrong, creating a contradiction between the documented call-site and the actual implementation. This is a minor fidelity risk that the plan itself flags; it cannot be resolved without codebase access.
findings.suggested_edit: Either (a) resolve the audit inline before publishing the plan (preferred), or (b) mark the subprocess block explicitly as 'tentative — subject to audit result' and add an AC that verifies the final invocation matches write-reviewer-findings.sh line-for-line.

findings.location: Scope Boundary section and File Impact table
Implementation Notes → 'New Named Tuple _SchemaValidationResult'
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `_SchemaValidationResult` NamedTuple used at exactly one call-site (the single `_validate_findings_schema()` → `main()` path). A plain 3-tuple or even three named constants would carry the same information with less ceremony. The Rule-of-Three caution applies: the abstraction is not yet reused by S-B or S-C (those are explicitly out of scope here). Whether the NamedTuple earns its keep depends on whether S-B/S-C will import and pattern-match on it — if yes, it is justified; if S-B will re-wrap or re-define the result, the abstraction is premature. This cannot be confirmed without the S-B/S-C designs.
findings.suggested_edit: If S-B and S-C are already designed and will import `_SchemaValidationResult` directly, add a brief note in the plan explaining that cross-story reuse is the motivation. If they are not yet designed, consider deferring the NamedTuple until the second story and using a simpler return value (e.g., a dataclass or plain tuple) for S-A alone.

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'validator_error path emits CRITICAL to stderr and returns non-zero from main()' and 'All T1 tests now pass (GREEN after implementation)'
findings.finding: Most ACs are mechanically verifiable (grep checks, import smoke tests, pytest invocations). However, two ACs have gaps: (1) The 'validator_error path' AC only runs a structural mock check and explicitly comments 'full main() integration tested in T1 tests' — the T1 tests are referenced but not defined anywhere in the plan, meaning the agent must locate or author them without guidance. (2) The 'All T1 tests now pass' AC assumes pre-existing tests tagged test_validate_findings_schema, test_main_validator_error, test_main_schema_fail exist in test_runner_smoke.py; if they don't exist yet, the AC is vacuously unverifiable (pytest will report 0 tests collected and exit 0, giving a false pass). The plan says 'Testing Mode: UPDATE' but provides no test authoring instructions or stubs.
findings.suggested_edit: Either (a) add a 'write these test stubs' section listing the three test functions with their required assertions, or (b) change the T1 AC to an explicit 'author and pass' check: 'pytest … -k … | grep -E "3 passed"' so zero-collected is not a false positive. Document that T1 tests must be created as part of this task.

findings.location: Implementation Notes → 'Subprocess invocation' section vs. Acceptance Criteria → 'validate-review-output.sh is invoked with code-review-dispatch as first arg'
findings.finding: Two minor coherence issues: (1) The plan body instructs the sub-agent to read write-reviewer-findings.sh and validate-review-output.sh to confirm exact invocation details (a MUST-read step), but there is no corresponding AC that verifies the agent actually audited these files or that the resulting invocation matches the shell script. The sole AC verifies only that the string 'code-review-dispatch' appears, not cwd, env vars, or stdin vs file semantics. This leaves the 'single-source-of-truth invariant' audit underchecked. (2) The pattern reference quotes: '"$SCRIPT_DIR/validate-review-output.sh" code-review-dispatch "$PENDING_FILE" >&2' — the >&2 redirect hints that the validator's stdout may go to stderr in the shell caller, but the subprocess.run in the plan uses capture_output=True (captures both). This is not flagged as intentional divergence anywhere, which is a minor inconsistency between the reference pattern and the prescribed implementation.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an AC: 'Confirm invocation matches write-reviewer-findings.sh: cwd defaults to plugin_root, no required env vars injected beyond inherited environment, stdout/stderr both captured (capture_output=True).' Also explicitly note in Implementation Notes that the >&2 redirect in the shell script is a caller-side concern and capture_output=True is intentional for Python.

findings.location: Implementation Notes → 'Subprocess invocation' — 'IMPORTANT: Before finalizing the invocation, the sub-agent MUST read … write-reviewer-findings.sh and … validate-review-output.sh'
findings.finding: The plan explicitly defers a real design decision to the executor: it prescribes a specific subprocess invocation but then says the agent MUST audit two external shell scripts to confirm whether the args order, stdin vs file semantics, and required env vars are correct, and potentially override the prescribed implementation. This is an unresolved decision-deferral — the plan's subprocess snippet may be wrong and the agent must discover that at runtime. If the scripts reveal a different invocation (e.g., stdin instead of file, different arg order, required CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT injection), the agent must deviate from the plan with no guidance on how far deviation is permitted. This is an 'explicit decision-deferral' that pushes a real design decision onto the executor.
findings.suggested_edit: The plan author should read the two shell scripts before publishing and encode the confirmed invocation directly in the plan. Replace the IMPORTANT note with a resolved statement like: 'Confirmed from audit of write-reviewer-findings.sh (line X): args order is prompt-id first, file second; file input (not stdin); no additional env vars required beyond inherited PATH.' If the scripts cannot be audited pre-publication, at minimum add a decision rule: 'If audit reveals stdin input is required, use input=json_string and omit tmpfile; if env var CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is required, inject it from os.environ.'

findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'All T1 tests now pass (GREEN after implementation)' and 'schema_fail path continues (does NOT emit CRITICAL, does NOT return 1)'
findings.finding: The end-state is largely well-defined and the ACs cover the main DD items. (2) The schema_fail AC uses a grep heuristic ('grep -v CRITICAL|return 1 | grep -q schema_fail') that checks for the absence of CRITICAL and return 1 in a 10-line window — this static grep cannot actually verify runtime behavior (a comment line mentioning 'schema_fail' would satisfy it). Replace the grep-based AC with one that actually imports and exercises the code path.
