findings.location: SC2: 'ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml' and SC3: 'required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset'
findings.finding: The plan requires fail-closed token/precondition checks to be replicated across three separate CI workflow files, and it maintains a file-based 'required-checks.txt' that must be kept in sync with the live GitHub ruleset. Both patterns carry NIH/proliferation risk: (a) duplicating the same fail-closed wrapper logic across three YAML files rather than a single shared composite action or reusable workflow could violate Rule-of-Three and create drift; (b) a file-based mirror of the live ruleset is a second source of truth that must be manually kept in sync and whose staleness is the exact failure mode the R8 guard is meant to catch — introducing complexity to guard complexity. Cannot confirm without inspecting the existing CI files whether a shared action already exists or whether required-checks.txt was pre-existing.
findings.suggested_edit: If the fail-closed token-check logic is not already in a shared composite action, consider extracting it to one (e.g., .github/actions/fail-closed-preflight/action.yml) and referencing it from all three carriers. For required-checks.txt, confirm whether GitHub's API can be queried directly in the R8 guard instead of maintaining a file mirror; if a file mirror is truly needed, document the authoritative-source decision in ADR-0020.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA: SC1, SC2, SC3, SC4, SC5, SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub'
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria explicitly require live GitHub operations, real PR2 traffic, and real ruleset state that cannot be completed or verified within a single automated session without persistent external state and human-operated infrastructure. Specifically: SC1 requires 'Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED (not skip-to-success)'; SC2 requires verifying that a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED 'in ALL THREE carriers' on a live system; SC3 requires verifying 'live ruleset enforces it, durably' and checking 'live ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'; SC5 requires 'a docs-only PR now requires review; an image/lockfile PR still skips' on live traffic. The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES explicitly state 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub.' These cannot be simulated or stubbed — they require actual GitHub ruleset mutation, real PAT credentials (with specific scopes), and live PR merges. No criterion provides a simulation or offline equivalent path. The named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, etc.) are opaque references with no in-plan definition of what they entail, leaving an agent unable to confirm completion without external docs. SC6's 'ADR-0020 updated with execution results' similarly depends on outcomes only known post-execution.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add an offline-verifiable proxy: e.g., for SC1, a scripted mock of the GitHub Checks API response asserting the gate returns failure when coverage is absent; for SC2, a unit/integration test that calls the CI wrapper with a revoked-token fixture and asserts non-zero exit; for SC3, a snapshot-comparison of required-checks.txt against a fixture. Define each E-series/V-series experiment inline (inputs, expected outputs, pass/fail threshold) so an agent can run them deterministically. Separate 'agent-completable verification steps' from 'human live-ops sign-off' gates, and mark the latter explicitly as out-of-scope for automated execution.

findings.location: Plan body: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel'; SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1-SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
findings.finding:
1. ORPHAN CRITERIA REFERENCES: SC1 cites [E2/E13], SC2 cites [V0.3a-post-fix, R8 fail-closed], SC3 cites [E6', V0.11], SC4 cites [E5/E8/E14/E15], SC5 cites [E11/E-compute-hash] — none of these experiment/version identifiers are defined anywhere in the plan body. They appear in referenced external docs (option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020) but not in this ticket. An agent relying solely on this plan cannot map these references to concrete work.
2. DESCRIBED WORK WITH NO CRITERION: The plan body mentions 'stories 1-4', 'story 5', 'story 6', 'story 7', 'story 8' with dependency relationships, but no story content, scope, or acceptance criteria are listed. The plan describes an 'epic' decomposed into stories, yet none of the stories are defined here — their individual acceptance criteria, scope, and deliverables are entirely absent.
3. TERMINOLOGY INCONSISTENCY: The gate is called 'review-gate' in SC1/SC3, 'LLM review' in the NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, 'llm-review' in SC3, and 'review-coverage-invariant' in SC2. It is unclear whether 'llm-review' (the check retained per SC3) and 'review-gate' (the new required check per SC1) are distinct CI jobs or synonyms — the plan uses both in the same sentence ('requires review-gate and retains llm-review') without explaining the relationship.
4. NEAR-DUPLICATE REQUIREMENTS: SC1's 'fail-closes unless every SHA in origin/main..head is proven reviewed' and SC2's 'all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed on precondition/token failure' overlap in describing fail-closed behavior without clearly partitioning which condition each owns.
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Inline the definition of each E/V/R experiment as a numbered sub-list under the SC that cites it, specifying: input fixture, command/script, and pass criterion. 2. Add a story-breakdown section listing stories 1-8 with at minimum a one-sentence scope and their individual ACs. 3. Add a 'Terminology' section disambiguating: review-gate (the new ruleset-required check job name), llm-review (the retained legacy check), review-coverage-invariant (the CI wrapper yml), and their relationships. 4. Separate SC1 into 'gate existence/wiring' and SC2 into 'fail-closed token handling' with no overlapping language.

findings.location: SEQUENCING: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 (UX) is independent'; SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1-SC6
findings.finding:
The plan references 8 stories and multiple phases but defines NONE of them in the plan body. An executing agent has no actionable work items: it cannot determine what files to create/modify, what CI YAML to write, what ruleset API calls to make, or what the enforce-flip entails — all of this is deferred to external documents (option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020) that are not reproduced here. This forces the agent to stop and locate/interpret external documents before taking any action, violating the 'executable without clarification' standard.

Additional ambiguities that would cause an agent to stop:
- 'the enforcing checks must fail-closed (never silently green on a precondition/token/expiry failure)' — no definition of what constitutes a 'precondition failure' vs. a normal skip path.
- 'a commit whose diff is entirely within the event-sourced ticket store is exempt' (SC4) — 'event-sourced ticket store' is not defined; its filesystem path/glob is unspecified.
- 'behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config)' vs. 'genuinely-inert artifacts (images, lockfiles, generated)' (SC5) — exact glob patterns for the allowlist are not given; 'skills' and 'behavior-driving config' are undefined.
- 'the single enforce-flip (final story)' — the mechanism (ruleset API call? YAML change? both?) is not described.
- 'admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads' — which PAT is available in which context; how it is injected into CI is not specified.
- 'land the swap with no other PR2 in flight' (DELIVERY PRINCIPLES) — 'PR2' is used throughout but never defined in this plan (appears to be a named convention from the external doc).
findings.suggested_edit: Expand the plan to include, inline: (a) the full story definitions (scope, files changed, commands run) for stories 1-8; (b) the glob/path definitions for the ticket store, allowlist, and behavior-bearing surfaces; (c) the exact mechanism of the enforce-flip (which API endpoint or YAML key changes, in what order, with what rollback step); (d) a glossary entry for 'PR2' and 'PR1'; (e) the token injection pattern (secret name, env var, CI context). At minimum, the external docs sections relevant to each story should be quoted or summarized so the agent does not need to leave this ticket.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA: 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: 'Warn-first: every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip'
findings.finding:
1. TERMINATION CONDITION IS UNDERSPECIFIED: The epic closes when 'ALL [SC1-SC6] are met + completion-verifier PASS.' The 'completion-verifier' is never defined — its inputs, script location, invocation command, and pass/fail output format are absent. An agent cannot know when to declare the work done.
2. WARN→ENFORCE TRANSITION GAP: The plan mandates a warn-first approach ('validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip') but does not specify: how long warn mode must run, what traffic volume or PR count constitutes sufficient validation, or what observable signals (zero false-positives? N consecutive clean runs?) authorize the enforce-flip. This is an open decision deferred to the executor.
3. STORY VERIFICATION MISSING: Stories 1-8 have no individual done-criteria, so there is no intermediate way to verify progress before reaching the final enforce-flip. The dependency chain ('story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified') implies per-story verification, but no such criteria exist in the plan.
4. COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGERS REFERENCED BUT NOT DEFINED: 'Each story names a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' — but since no story content is given, no triggers are defined. This leaves the agent with no stop condition other than complete failure.
5. END-STATE REACHABILITY GAP: The union of described steps (warn mode → live verify → enforce-flip → UX story) does not fully cover SC6 ('ADR-0020 updated with execution results') because execution results are only known after the enforce-flip runs on live traffic — a post-hoc update that has no step defined for it.
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Define the completion-verifier: its script path, what it checks (e.g., required-checks.txt contents, live ruleset API response, ledger file hash), and its expected stdout/exit code. 2. Add a warn-mode validation threshold: e.g., '5 real PR2 runs with zero false-positives AND zero false-negatives over 48 hours, confirmed by log grep.' 3. Add per-story done-criteria as a checklist. 4. Add a concrete course-correction trigger per story (e.g., 'if warn-mode emits a false-positive block on a clean reviewed PR, halt and re-examine coverage ledger logic'). 5. Add an explicit step in story 6 or 7 for 'update ADR-0020 §execution-results section with observed outcomes.'

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT paragraph + SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1-SC6
SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces … are removed from the review-gate allowlist') vs. SC6 ('UX correctness fixes land — descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle')
findings.finding: SC5 (allowlist restructuring) and SC6 (flow UX fixes + doc updates) feel like scope additions relative to the title 'Implement Option A: ruleset-enforced fail-closed review gate on the two-tier flow'. The title promises a hardening of the gate mechanism; allowlist policy changes (SC5) and PR-title/sprint-umbrella UX fixes (SC6) are separable concerns. Without access to the referenced source docs (option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020) it cannot be confirmed whether these are explicitly part of Option A's definition or later additions. If they are out-of-scope additions, the plan over-delivers relative to the title, risking scope creep in execution.
findings.suggested_edit: Add one sentence in the SEQUENCING or SC5/SC6 sections explicitly tracing these items back to a numbered section in option-a-pivot-plan.md (e.g. 'SC5 implements Phase 2 §5.3; SC6 implements §5.5'). This makes the fidelity auditable without codebase access.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 (UX) is independent'
findings.finding: The plan names 8 child stories and a complex dependency graph, but the actual story definitions (scope, AC, implementation steps) are not included in or linked from this plan — only the story numbers and dependency edges are referenced. As an epic this is appropriate in spirit, but each story is load-bearing (each has its own 'warn-first' validation, named experiments, and a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER). Without the individual story breakdowns being present or linked, a reviewing agent cannot confirm that any single story is small enough to execute coherently in one session. Specifically: stories 1-4 being parallelisable suggests they are independently sized, but with no story bodies visible, it is impossible to validate that they are actually leaf-sized or that the dependency edges are correctly drawn (e.g., story 5 depending on 1+2+4 but not 3 is an unusual asymmetry with no stated rationale).
findings.suggested_edit: Either (a) attach or link stub story definitions for each of the 8 stories directly in this plan (even one-paragraph summaries with their own AC), or (b) add a table here that for each story lists: short scope statement, key deliverable, and why story 3 is not a dependency of story 5. This makes the decomposition auditable and prevents an agent from conflating epic-level AC with a single executable task.

findings.location: SC2 ('fail-closed on precondition/token failure … in ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml') and SC3 ('R8 live-parity guard fail-closes under enforce so PAT expiry/scope-strip/drift cannot silently disable it')
findings.finding: The plan introduces at least four distinct CI wrapper/guard mechanisms: review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml, and an 'R8 live-parity guard'. Each is described as independently fail-closing on precondition/token failure. Without codebase access it cannot be confirmed whether (a) a shared fail-closed precondition helper already exists or could serve all three carriers via a single entry-point (Rule-of-Three: three call-sites would justify a shared abstraction), or (b) each workflow really does duplicate the same token-check/expiry logic. If the token-check logic is copy-pasted into each yml independently, this is a proliferation risk — a future token rotation would require touching four separate files. Additionally, the plan does not address whether GitHub's native 'required status checks' already provide the fail-closed behavior for token/precondition failures at the ruleset level, potentially making the in-workflow guards partially redundant.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a note stating whether a shared composite action or reusable workflow handles the fail-closed precondition check across all three carriers. If not, call it out explicitly as a known duplication accepted for isolation reasons. Also confirm whether GitHub's ruleset behavior on workflow errors already satisfies the fail-closed requirement natively before implementing custom wrappers.

findings.location: SC1, SC2, SC3, SC4, SC5, SC6 and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub')
findings.finding: Multiple success criteria require live GitHub operations that cannot be completed within a single agent session without real credentials and a live repo: SC1 requires a real PR2 to be BLOCKED by the live ruleset ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'); SC2 requires triggering CI with a scope-stripped/expired token against live runners; SC3 requires verifying the live `main` ruleset and required-checks.txt drift; SC6 requires observing 'real PR2 traffic'. None of these can be completed by an agent in-session using simulation. The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES explicitly state 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)' with no simulated-environment fallback defined. Additionally, 'warn-first' validation on 'live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip' is an explicit out-of-session gate. No criterion is measurable without live GitHub state, and no criterion is reducible to a purely local/unit-testable check.
findings.suggested_edit: For each success criterion, add a split: (a) an in-session verifiable sub-criterion (e.g., unit tests, dry-run output, fixture-based CI run, or a script that exit-codes correctly on a local git repo with a stub ruleset) and (b) a clearly labeled out-of-session live-verify step that is explicitly owned by a human operator. This decouples what an agent can ship from what a human must sign off on, and keeps the agent's completion verifiable.

findings.location: SC1 references 'E2/E13'; SC2 references 'V0.3a-post-fix, R8 fail-closed'; SC3 references 'E6\', V0.11'; SC4 references 'E5/E8/E14/E15'; SC5 references 'E11/E-compute-hash'; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES references 'E-series/V-series experiments'
findings.finding: Every success criterion cites experiment/validation identifiers (E2, E13, V0.3a-post-fix, E6', V0.11, E5, E8, E14, E15, E11, E-compute-hash, R8) and story identifiers (1–8) that are defined only in an external document (`docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md`) not reproduced in this ticket. An agent executing solely from this ticket cannot determine what E2, V0.3a-post-fix, R8, or 'E-compute-hash' require without consulting that external source of truth. Additionally, the plan uses both 'review-gate' and 'llm-review' as check names, and also refers to 'merge-pipeline-checks', 'review-coverage-invariant.yml', 'dangling-references.yml', 'ruleset-invariants.yml' — the mapping between these names and the underlying GitHub required-check names is not explained in the ticket body, creating terminology ambiguity. SC6 mentions 'UX correctness fixes (descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle)' with no corresponding described work in the plan body (no criterion covers what 'correct' looks like for PR titles or the draft-umbrella lifecycle).
findings.suggested_edit: Inline the definitions of every cited experiment (E-series/V-series) and story (1–8) directly into the ticket, or attach them as appendices. Add a terminology glossary mapping each check name to its GitHub status-check string. Expand SC6 to specify what 'descriptive PR1 titles' and 'sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' mean concretely (e.g., a regex pattern for title validation, or lifecycle states to enforce).

findings.location: Multiple sections: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent'), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Warn-first: every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode'), NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, and all SC citations to external experiment IDs
findings.finding: An executing agent cannot act on this plan without stopping for clarification on several points: (1) The stories (1–8) referenced throughout are not defined in this ticket — the agent does not know what work each story entails, what files to create/modify, or what the warn-mode vs. enforce-mode implementation looks like. (2) The 'enforce-flip' (story 7) is described as 'the only hard-to-reverse step' with a runbook reference ('land the swap with no other PR2 in flight — R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window') but the runbook itself is not included, so the agent cannot execute it safely. (3) The COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER is mentioned ('the observable that, if seen, means stop and re-plan') but no observable is actually named — every story is said to 'name' one, but none are listed in this ticket. (4) The allowlist referenced in SC1 ('allowlisted-only PR2') and SC5 ('removed from the review-gate allowlist') is never defined — what paths are on it? (5) 'Per-SHA coverage' (SC1) and 'coverage ledger warm-hits' (SC4) are not explained mechanically — an agent would have to invent an implementation.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline each story's scope (files to create/modify, logic to implement, warn vs. enforce flag location) into the ticket. Include the runbook for story 7 inline. Name the specific course-correction observable for at least story 7 (the high-risk step). Define the allowlist (path globs or file name) and the per-SHA coverage ledger schema explicitly.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA preamble ('epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'), SEQUENCING section, and SC1–SC6 individually
findings.finding: Three gaps between described steps and stated end-state: (1) Termination is undefined for the agent: the plan says 'epic closes only when ALL [SC] are met + completion-verifier PASS' but the 'completion-verifier' is not described — no script name, no check list, no invocation command. An agent cannot know when it is done. (2) The sequencing (stories 1–8) is described only in terms of dependency ordering, not in terms of what constitutes 'story N done' — so even if an agent executes all steps reachable from this ticket, it cannot verify it has reached 'story N complete' before unlocking story N+1. (3) SC3 requires both 'required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset' to reflect the swap, but the plan describes no mechanism for keeping required-checks.txt in sync with the live ruleset (no script, no CI job, no manual step), leaving a gap between the file artifact and the live state. SC6 says 'docs reflect the final enforced architecture; ADR-0020 updated with execution results' but specifies no concrete doc sections or ADR fields that must change, making this unmeasurable.
findings.suggested_edit: Define the completion-verifier explicitly (e.g., a script path and expected exit code, or a checklist of observable states). For each story, add a 'Definition of Done' sub-list. Add a step (script or CI job) that reads the live ruleset via GitHub API and diffs it against required-checks.txt, failing if they diverge. For SC6, name the specific doc sections and ADR fields that must be updated.

findings.location: SC1 ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'), SC2 ('Verified: a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED'), SC3 ('the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset')
findings.finding: Multiple success criteria require live GitHub validation ('Verified live', 'live ruleset', 'live PR2 traffic') that cannot be completed within a single agent working session without real GitHub admin access, real PATs, and real PR traffic. The plan itself acknowledges 'admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads' as a precondition but does not guarantee these are pre-provisioned or simulable. SC1 requires an actual BLOCKED PR2 to be observable; SC2 requires a real token to be stripped/expired against live CI runners; SC3 requires a live ruleset read-back from the GitHub API. These are not automatable in isolation — they depend on external system state that an agent cannot fabricate or simulate. Additionally, the plan states 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub … AND by the named E-series/V-series experiments' as a delivery principle, making live ops a first-class requirement rather than optional. The warn-first/enforce-flip sequence ('validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip') further embeds a human-paced temporal gate between stories that cannot be compressed into one session. SC4's 'no flaky-check team block' and SC6's 'docs reflect the final enforced architecture' are somewhat measurable but rely on the same live-verification chain.
findings.suggested_edit: For each live-verification requirement, add an explicit 'in-session surrogate': e.g., a dry-run GitHub API call against a fork/test repo with a provided PAT, a local Docker simulation of the CI runner, or a recorded-fixture replay. Separate what an agent CAN verify automatically (file content, YAML syntax, ruleset JSON shape, script logic) from what requires human sign-off (live BLOCKED PR observation, live token-expiry test). Mark the latter as manual-acceptance steps outside the agent's scope and remove them from the agent-executable acceptance criteria.

findings.location: SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config)'); plan body ('two-tier flow', 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap'); ADR-0020 reference
findings.finding: Most success criteria (SC1-SC6) do map to described delivery principles and the non-negotiable requirement, but there are two coherence gaps and one terminology inconsistency worth flagging: (1) SC5 introduces the concept of 'behavior-bearing surfaces' and 'event-sourced ticket store' with no definition or enumeration anywhere in the plan body — an executor cannot know which paths/files constitute these categories without consulting external documents (option-a-pivot-plan.md / ADR-0020) that are referenced but not summarized. (2) The plan body names 'two-tier flow' and 'review-gate' consistently, but 'merge-pipeline-checks' appears only in SC3 without being defined in the plan body — it is unclear whether this is the name of an existing GitHub check, a YAML workflow job, or a required-checks entry. (3) Stories 1-8 are referenced repeatedly in sequencing ('story 5 depends on 1+2+4') but the stories themselves are not included in the plan — the plan body refers to an external decomposition that the reviewer/executor cannot see. This makes the described work partially orphaned from its criteria.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a one-paragraph glossary inline (or a 'Key terms' section) defining: 'two-tier flow', 'merge-pipeline-checks' (current check name), 'review-gate' (new check name), 'event-sourced ticket store' (path glob), 'behavior-bearing surface' (path glob list). Inline at least a skeleton of stories 1-8 (name + one-line scope) so the sequencing constraints are self-contained.

findings.location: Multiple locations: 'behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config)' (SC5); 'per-SHA coverage' (SC1); 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs' (SC4); 'R8 live-parity guard' (SC3); stories 1-8 (sequencing section)
findings.finding: An executing agent would need to stop and ask clarifying questions at several points: (1) SC5's allowlist split between 'behavior-bearing' and 'genuinely-inert' is not operationalized — 'skills', 'behavior-driving config' are named but no file paths, glob patterns, or discovery algorithm is provided. An agent cannot implement an allowlist without knowing what goes on which side. (2) 'Per-SHA coverage' (SC1) does not define what constitutes 'proven reviewed' — is it a signed artifact, a ledger entry, a label on a commit, a database record? The storage format and lookup mechanism are unspecified. (3) 'Coverage ledger warm-hits' (SC4) implies a caching mechanism whose schema, location, and invalidation rules are entirely unspecified. (4) 'R8 live-parity guard fail-closes' (SC3) — R8 is referenced as a known concept but is not defined in the plan; an agent would need to consult external docs to understand what guard to implement. (5) The COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER mentioned in the delivery principles is never defined per-story — the plan says 'each story names a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' but no story content is present in the plan. (6) The enforce-flip runbook ('land the swap with no other PR2 in flight') is described in one sentence but has no concrete procedure — what command/API call performs the swap, how is the 'no other PR2 in flight' precondition checked?
findings.suggested_edit: For each underspecified element: (a) SC5 allowlist — provide an explicit glob list (e.g., `docs/**`, `skills/**`, `config/behavior*.yml`) and the exact file/section that will carry it. (b) SC1 'proven reviewed' — specify the artifact (e.g., a signed JSON ledger at `.github/review-ledger/<sha>.json` with fields `sha`, `reviewer`, `timestamp`, `signature`). (c) SC4 ledger caching — specify the cache key and storage location. (d) R8 — inline its definition or add a one-paragraph summary. (e) Enforce-flip runbook — provide the exact API call or `gh` CLI command sequence.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA section; SEQUENCING section ('story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified'); 'completion-verifier PASS'
findings.finding: The end-state verification mechanism has two gaps: (1) 'completion-verifier PASS' is invoked as the final gate in the success criteria preamble but the completion-verifier is never defined — its inputs, outputs, invocation command, and pass/fail conditions are entirely absent. An agent cannot know when the epic is done if the verifier is a black box. (2) The UNION of described steps does not fully reach SC2: SC2 requires fail-closed behavior in THREE specific workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml), but the plan body describes changes to the gate logic in general terms without specifying that all three files will be modified, what the current exit-code behavior is in each, or what the specific code change is. If only one or two files are updated, SC2 would not be met and there is no checklist to catch the gap. (3) The termination condition for the warn→enforce transition ('validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip') is not time-bounded or quantified — 'validated' is undefined (how many PRs? over what period? by whom?). An agent in warn mode has no signal to stop waiting and flip.
findings.suggested_edit: (1) Define the completion-verifier inline: name the script/command, its expected stdout/exit-code, and the conditions it checks against SC1-SC6. (2) Add a per-file checklist under SC2 explicitly enumerating what change is needed in each of the three YML files (e.g., 'change `exit 0` on line N to `exit 1` when TOKEN env var is empty'). (3) Replace 'validated on live PR2 traffic' with a concrete, agent-checkable threshold (e.g., 'at least 3 PRs processed with 0 false-positives over 48 h, recorded in warn-validation-log.md') so the transition trigger is unambiguous.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub … AND by the named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The plan references a rich set of named experiments (E2, E5, E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash, E6') as test evidence, but the individual story bodies are not included in the ticket text, so it is impossible to confirm that: (a) each experiment has a defined RED-before-GREEN step, (b) failure-path cases (scope-stripped token, expired PAT, drift) are exercised before the warn→enforce flip rather than only described in SC2, and (c) the 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs' assertion (SC4) has a concrete repeatability test. Without the story-level details, these may be fully specified or may be absent. The plan text also does not mention who runs the experiments or what constitutes a recorded PASS artifact — success is self-certified by reading live GitHub state rather than a reproducible assertion.
findings.suggested_edit: For each experiment cited in SC1–SC6, add a one-line definition of: observable (what is measured), expected value (RED/GREEN), and where the result is recorded (e.g. docs/handoff/experiment-log.md). At minimum, confirm that SC2's three-carrier fail-closed check is exercised with a deliberately broken token BEFORE story 7 (enforce-flip) is unlocked.

findings.location: SC1 ('a single always-runs required check `review-gate` exists'), SC3 ('live `main` ruleset requires `review-gate` and retains `llm-review`'), SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces … are removed from the review-gate allowlist')
findings.finding: Several structural premises are asserted as facts but cannot be confirmed from plan text alone against the live codebase/GitHub configuration: (1) that a `review-gate` check does not already exist (or exists but is wired differently) — if it exists today the story's creation step would be a no-op or a conflict; (2) that `llm-review` is currently a required check on the ruleset and is currently retained (SC3 says 'retains `llm-review`' implying it pre-exists); (3) that the current allowlist actually contains the behavior-bearing surfaces named in SC5 (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config) — if any of these are already exempt or already required the story scope changes; (4) that a 'per-SHA coverage ledger' mechanism already exists in review-coverage-invariant.yml (SC1/SC4 depend on it). None of these are obviously wrong, but each is a live-codebase assertion the executor must verify before writing code.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Prerequisite Checks' subsection (one story or a story-0 spike) that explicitly verifies: current required-checks.txt contents, current ruleset required checks in GitHub, current allowlist file path and entries, and whether review-coverage-invariant.yml already implements per-SHA ledger logic. Gate story 1 on this verification.

findings.location: SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'), SC3 ('required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'), SC5 ('removed from the review-gate allowlist'), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('docs reflect the final enforced architecture; ADR-0020 updated')
findings.finding: The plan names several concrete file targets (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml, required-checks.txt, docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md, the allowlist file) which is good specificity, but: (a) the allowlist file is never named by path — it is referenced only as 'review-gate allowlist', so the executor must discover it; (b) no caller/consumer inventory is given for required-checks.txt — if other workflows parse or import this file, they need updating when the merge-pipeline-checks→review-gate swap happens; (c) the 'behavior-driving config' category in SC5 is not enumerated — an executor will have to make a judgment call about what qualifies, which risks scope drift or an incomplete flip; (d) story 8 (UX: descriptive PR1 titles, sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle) implies changes to PR creation scripts/templates, but no file targets are named for those.
findings.suggested_edit: (a) Name the allowlist file by its full repo path. (b) Add a note listing any other workflows or scripts that read required-checks.txt so the swap story knows its full blast radius. (c) In SC5, enumerate the specific paths or glob patterns that constitute 'behavior-driving config' to remove ambiguity. (d) For story 8, name the script/template files that produce PR1 titles and the draft-umbrella lifecycle.

findings.location: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent') vs. SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces … are removed from the review-gate allowlist') and SC3 ('the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset')
findings.finding: Two coherence tensions exist: (1) SC5 requires that the allowlist be modified (removing behavior-bearing surfaces) and SC3 requires the live ruleset swap — both of these must be complete before the enforce-flip (story 7), yet story 5 is described as depending only on stories 1+2+4, and story 6 on story 5. SC5 and SC3 span what appear to be stories 5/6/maybe 3, but the mapping of SC-numbers to story-numbers is never made explicit, leaving it unclear whether SC5's allowlist change is gated correctly. (2) The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES state 'warn-first: every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip', yet SC2 requires that three CI wrappers 'fail-closed' — this is stated as an already-achieved condition to be verified (V0.3a-post-fix), not something landed in warn mode first. If the wrappers already fail-closed and story 7 only flips the ruleset, this is consistent; but if any wrapper still needs a code change to fail-close, that change would violate the warn-first principle. The plan does not disambiguate.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a story→SC mapping table (e.g. 'Story 3 delivers SC3; Story 4 delivers SC5; …'). For SC2, explicitly state whether the three-carrier fail-closed behavior is pre-existing (already landed in a prior phase) or must be landed as part of this epic, and if the latter, identify which story delivers it and confirm it is gated before story 7.

findings.location: SC1–SC6 (all success criteria), 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub', 'COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER', 'enforce-flip story carries the runbook (land the swap with no other PR2 in flight)'
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria require live, out-of-session validation that an autonomous agent cannot perform in a single session: SC1 requires 'Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED (not skip-to-success)'; SC2 requires running with a 'scope-stripped/expired token' against live CI; SC3 requires verifying a 'live ruleset'; the enforce-flip (story 7) requires 'no other PR2 in flight' — a real-time org-state precondition. None of these can be completed by an agent executing offline or against a mock environment without real GitHub admin access and actual PR traffic. There is also no simulation/stub substitute defined for any of these. Additionally, 'COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER: the observable that, if seen, means stop and re-plan' is named as a named element in every story but is entirely absent from the acceptance criteria and body, leaving measurability undefined.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC that requires live validation, add an explicit in-session proxy: e.g., 'Verified by: running `scripts/verify-gate.sh --dry-run` against a recorded fixture of GitHub API responses' or 'acceptance by code review of the logic path; live validation is out-of-band and tracked in a separate ops runbook story.' Mark the live-verification steps as post-merge ops tasks outside the agent's story scope.

findings.location: SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces … removed from the review-gate allowlist'); SC6 ('UX correctness fixes land — descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle'); DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('story 8 (UX) is independent'); NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT ('every commit reaching main must pass an LLM review')
findings.finding: Two coherence gaps: (1) SC6 lumps two distinct concerns — 'flow preserved' and 'docs/ADR updated' — into one criterion with separate verifiers but no story-level mapping is given in the visible plan body, making it impossible to confirm no orphan work. (2) 'Story 8 (UX)' is mentioned in SEQUENCING but no corresponding acceptance criterion exists for the UX story specifically; SC6 partially covers it but the draft-umbrella lifecycle / descriptive PR1 titles have no measurable done-state. Terminology is mostly consistent ('review-gate', 'llm-review', 'PR2'), though 'merge-pipeline-checks' appears only once (SC3) and is never defined or cross-referenced in the plan body, creating a potential orphan term.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a dedicated SC7 for story 8's UX deliverables (e.g., 'PR1 titles follow pattern X; sprint umbrella draft closes on merge verified by Y'). Define 'merge-pipeline-checks' on first use and cross-reference it to the CI file it corresponds to.

findings.location: 'docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md (CONVERGED, review #1-6 + experiments V0.1-V0.12)'; 'per-SHA coverage'; 'R8 live-parity guard'; 'coverage ledger warm-hits'; 'event-sourced ticket store'; SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start')
findings.finding: An executing agent is told to treat 'docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md' and 'docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md' as source-of-truth for Phase 2 (§5) and prerequisites, but the plan does not reproduce, summarize, or inline ANY of the concrete steps/artifacts from those documents. Key implementation-bearing concepts — 'per-SHA coverage', 'coverage ledger', 'R8 live-parity guard', 'event-sourced ticket store', 'allowlist', and the individual stories 1-8 with their COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGERs — are referenced but never defined in the ticket. If those external docs are unavailable or differ from the agent's working copy, the agent cannot proceed without clarifying. Additionally, SEQUENCING says 'story 5 depends on 1+2+4' but the stories themselves are not defined in the plan, leaving the dependency graph unresolvable without the external doc.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the story definitions (at minimum: story title, done criteria, COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER, dependency) from the referenced docs, or restructure as a pure epic with child tickets that each contain the full story. At minimum, define the 8 stories by name in the plan body so the agent has unambiguous scope per story.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA header ('epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'); 'completion-verifier PASS'; SC2 ('in ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'); SC3 ('reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset')
findings.finding: The plan defines a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the final gate but never specifies what the completion-verifier is, where it lives, or how it is invoked. This is the primary termination signal and it is undefined. Additionally, SC2 names three specific YAML files but the plan does not describe any step that creates or modifies these files, so there is no described path from current state to the end-state those criteria assert. SC3 requires 'required-checks.txt' to be updated but no step or story creating/updating that file is described. The gap between described work (which is almost entirely deferred to external docs) and the success criteria leaves the union of steps → end-state unverifiable.
findings.suggested_edit: Define the completion-verifier explicitly (e.g., 'run `scripts/completion-verifier.sh`; it exits 0 iff all six SCs pass their automated checks'). For each SC, add a 'How verified in-session' note pointing to a concrete script, test, or artifact diff rather than live GitHub state alone.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES; SC1
Title vs. plan body; SUCCESS CRITERIA; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
findings.finding: The plan body faithfully targets the stated title ('ruleset-enforced fail-closed review gate on the two-tier flow'). All six success criteria map directly to hardening, fail-closed behavior, or preserving the two-tier flow. Story 8 (UX fixes) is the closest to scope drift but is explicitly called out as complementary, not a distraction from the headline intent. No contradiction between headline and details was found.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub … AND by the named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The plan names a rich set of experiment/validation tags (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash) as the test evidence, but the definitions, preconditions, and pass/fail criteria of each experiment live entirely in the referenced external documents (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, docs/adr/0020). Without seeing those documents it is impossible to confirm: (a) whether RED-before-GREEN ordering is specified for E2/E13 (the primary BLOCK-not-skip test), (b) whether failure-path coverage (scope-stripped token → RED gate) is exercised in V0.3a-post-fix vs. assumed from a previous run, (c) whether boundary cases like the 'coverage ledger warm-hit across re-runs' (SC4/E14-E15) have a deterministic pass signal or rely on ad-hoc observation. The plan does name failure-path cases (silent-pass on precondition/token failure, expired-token, scope-strip) in SC2, which is positive. However, the actual test scripts, assertions, and RED-before-GREEN sequencing are not reproduced here — they are assumed to exist in the external source-of-truth. This is a plan-text ambiguity, not a confirmed gap.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a brief inline test-matrix table per story (or per SC) that states: the experiment tag, the observable signal that means PASS, the observable that means FAIL/stop, and whether the test must run in RED state first. This removes the dependency on external docs for reviewers and executors to assess test completeness.

findings.location: SC2: 'all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed … in ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3: 'live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'; SC5: 'behavior-bearing surfaces … are removed from the review-gate allowlist'
findings.finding:
The plan makes several assertions about the current state of the codebase/environment that an executor must verify before relying on them, but which cannot be confirmed from plan text alone:
1. SC2 asserts three specific YAML workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) are the complete and correct set of carriers. If a fourth enforcing wrapper exists, or if one of the three was renamed, the 'ALL THREE' coverage claim is silently incomplete.
2. SC3 asserts the live ruleset currently 'retains llm-review' and that required-checks.txt exists and is the canonical file for ruleset state. Both are environmental facts that could have drifted.
3. SC5 asserts that behavior-bearing surfaces are currently on the allowlist. If they were already removed in a prior experiment, the story becomes a no-op or double-application.
4. The plan references 'origin/main..head per-SHA coverage' (SC1) and a 'coverage ledger warm-hit' (SC4) as if these mechanisms already exist. If they do not yet exist and must be created by this epic, the plan needs to say so explicitly; if they do exist, their location is unverified.
5. 'admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads' — the plan assumes these PATs exist and have the stated scopes without citing where they are stored/verified.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a short 'Pre-flight verification checklist' section (or prefix each story with 'Assume verified:' items) that names each environmental assumption and the command/check that confirms it before the story is executed. E.g.: 'Verify workflow file names: `ls .github/workflows/ | grep -E "review-coverage|dangling|ruleset"`; expected: exactly these three files.'

findings.location: SC1–SC5 and SEQUENCING section; references to 'review-gate', 'merge-pipeline-checks', 'required-checks.txt', 'allowlist', 'coverage ledger', 'two-tier flow'
findings.finding:
The plan names several key artifacts but does not specify their file paths or symbols with enough precision to assess blast radius:
1. 'review-gate' check — is this a new GitHub Actions workflow file? A reusable workflow call? A composite action? The plan does not name a target file path (e.g., .github/workflows/review-gate.yml).
2. 'merge-pipeline-checks -> review-gate swap' (SC3) — 'merge-pipeline-checks' is a pre-existing required check that will be removed from the ruleset; the plan names no file or workflow corresponding to it, so callers/consumers (any workflow that depends on or is depended on by that check) are unacknowledged.
3. 'allowlist' (SC5) — the file path of the allowlist is not given. If it is embedded in a workflow YAML, the specific file and key must be named.
4. 'coverage ledger' (SC1/SC4) — not located. Is this a file committed to the repo, an artifact uploaded by CI, or an in-memory structure? Consumers of it (the review-gate check) need to be traceable.
5. 'required-checks.txt' (SC3) — present as a concept but its path and the tooling that reads/writes it are unspecified.
6. The SEQUENCING section says 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel' but does not name which files stories 1-4 actually touch, making it impossible to assess edit-set overlap/conflicts between parallel stories.
High blast radius (ruleset changes are repo-wide; SC3 explicitly flags R8 RED as repo-wide during skew window) is acknowledged in the reversibility note, which is positive.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Files/symbols changed' subsection per story (even a bullet list) naming the target workflow YAML paths, config files, and any scripts created or modified. For the ruleset swap specifically, name the exact GitHub API call or UI path and the existing required-check name being replaced.

findings.location: Multiple cross-section pairs (see findings below)
findings.finding:
Three internal tension points were identified:

1. WARN-FIRST vs. SC2 fail-closed assertion: The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES state 'every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip.' However, SC2 asserts 'all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed on precondition/token failure under enforce mode' as a success criterion verified across the epic — not deferred to story 7 (enforce-flip). It is ambiguous whether SC2 is verified only after the enforce-flip (story 7) or whether each intermediate story must already be fail-closed on preconditions even in warn mode. If the latter, the warn-first principle is effectively nullified for precondition failures; if the former, SC2 cannot be a gate for stories 1–6.

2. SEQUENCING parallelism vs. dependency graph: SEQUENCING states 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4.' SC3 (live ruleset swap) is mapped to story 6 which depends on story 5. But SC5 (allowlist changes, behavior-bearing surfaces become reviewable) must be in place before the enforce-flip (story 7) and also logically before the live-verify of SC1 (no-skip-to-success for un-reviewed content), because without allowlist changes a docs PR would silently skip. The plan does not clarify whether SC5/story addressing allowlist changes completes before or after the SC1 live-verify, creating a potential ordering ambiguity.

3. SC4 'no false wedge' vs. SC1 'no code_changed skip': SC1 requires the gate to run on EVERY base==main PR with no code_changed skip. SC4 exempts commits whose diff is 'entirely within the event-sourced ticket store.' These are logically compatible (the gate runs but exits-0 for exempt content) but the plan uses the word 'exempt' in SC4 and 'no skip' in SC1 without bridging the two. An executor could reasonably read SC4 as introducing a code_changed-style path condition that SC1 prohibits, leading to conflicting implementations.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Clarify in SC2 (or DELIVERY PRINCIPLES): 'fail-closed on precondition/token failure applies only after enforce-flip (story 7); in warn mode, precondition failure emits a warning annotation but does not exit non-zero.' OR flip it: 'even in warn mode, precondition failures must exit non-zero — only the review-result itself is advisory.' 
2. Add a note in SEQUENCING: 'Allowlist changes (SC5) must be verified before the SC1 live-verify in story X.' 
3. In SC1 add: 'Ticket-store-exempt commits are still evaluated by the gate; they exit-0 via the exemption path, not via a skip of the workflow step.'

findings.location: SEQUENCING section + implicit story references (stories 1–8)
SC2: 'ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3: 'R8 live-parity guard'
findings.finding: The plan introduces at least three separate CI workflow files plus a dedicated live-parity guard (R8) as independent carriers of the same fail-closed token-expiry logic. Without codebase access it is impossible to confirm whether a shared helper/composite action already centralises this pattern. If a single reusable action or script already exists, duplicating the fail-closed precondition check in three separate YAMLs is mild NIH/config proliferation. Additionally, the plan does not name whether a GitHub composite action or reusable workflow is used to DRY up the shared logic, which is the standard GitHub-native mechanism.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit note stating whether the fail-closed precondition check is factored into a single reusable composite action (preferred) or is intentionally duplicated per-carrier and why. If duplication is deliberate (e.g. isolation / blast-radius), document that rationale.

findings.location: SC1–SC5; E/V experiment references throughout; COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER per story; Warn-first principle

SC3: 'live `main` ruleset requires `review-gate` and retains `llm-review`'; SC1: 'a single always-runs required check `review-gate` exists'; SEQUENCING: 'story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified'
findings.finding: The plan asserts as a baseline that (a) a live ruleset currently exists on `main`, (b) it currently contains `llm-review` as a required check, and (c) the existing `merge-pipeline-checks` check exists and is swap-able for `review-gate`. These are environmental facts the plan never directs anyone to verify before execution begins. If any of these are absent or differently named in the live repo the sequencing collapses (story 5 swap target does not exist; story 7 gate cannot be validated). The plan also references 'required-checks.txt' as a file-on-disk source of truth but does not verify it currently exists or is up-to-date with the live ruleset.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Preconditions / Environment Check' section (or Story 0) that explicitly verifies: (1) the live main-branch ruleset exists and its current required-checks list; (2) `merge-pipeline-checks` exists as a check name in the ruleset; (3) `required-checks.txt` is present and matches the live ruleset; (4) `llm-review` check is currently in the ruleset. These should be scripted assertions (e.g. via GitHub API calls) before any story begins.

findings.location: SC1–SC5; 'docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md'; 'docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md'; 'review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'
findings.finding: The plan names three YML workflow files and two docs files explicitly, but the concrete file paths for the new `review-gate` check implementation, the coverage ledger, the allowlist configuration, and the 'required-checks.txt' are never stated. The 'merge-pipeline-checks -> review-gate swap' is described at a concept level but no file-level edit targets are specified for where the check name change lives (ruleset API call? a checked-in config file? both?). Without codebase access it is impossible to confirm these omissions versus deliberate deferral to child stories, but as written the plan's blast-radius is unacknowledged for the allowlist removal from SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces removed from the review-gate allowlist') — no file name for the allowlist is given.
findings.suggested_edit: Name the allowlist file/location explicitly (e.g. `.github/review-gate-allowlist.txt` or equivalent). State whether the ruleset required-checks update is performed via a checked-in Terraform/config file, a GitHub API call in a script, or both — and name that file. Even if child stories will detail further, the epic-level plan should name each primary artifact.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('warn-first … validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip'); SC4 ('coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs — no flaky-check team block'); SEQUENCING ('story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified')
findings.finding: One potential internal tension: SC4 requires the coverage ledger to 'warm-hit across re-runs' (implying a persistent cache), but the Warn-first / Reversibility principle and the hard-gated enforce-flip story suggest the ledger is only written/read during PR2 CI runs. The plan does not explain how ledger warm-hits are preserved across separate CI runs (cache key? artifact? external store?) in a way that is consistent with the reversibility/fail-closed guarantees. If the cache mechanism itself fails silently, SC4 and SC2 are in tension. This cannot be resolved without codebase access but the plan should acknowledge it.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a sentence under SC4 describing the ledger persistence mechanism (e.g. GitHub Actions cache with a deterministic key, or a committed file) and how a cache-miss is handled (fail-closed to avoid silent skip, or regenerate from scratch). Confirm this is consistent with the fail-closed guarantee in SC2.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub … AND by the named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The plan references a rich set of named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, E-compute-hash) as the primary test evidence for each SC. However, the definitions, procedures, expected RED/GREEN outputs, and pass/fail criteria for those experiments are declared to live in docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md and docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md — documents not included in this plan text. Without access to those documents it is impossible to confirm that the experiments actually cover: (a) per-SHA coverage boundary cases (SC1 gap: what happens to a PR with a mix of reviewed and unreviewed SHAs?), (b) explicit RED-before-GREEN ordering for the enforce-flip (story 7), and (c) token-expiry injection path for all three carriers independently (SC2). The plan's 'warn-first → enforce-flip' sequencing is a sound RED-before-GREEN structure at the story level, but whether individual experiment definitions enforce that at the check level cannot be confirmed from this text alone.
findings.suggested_edit: For each Success Criterion, inline a one-line test procedure (stimulus → expected observable) directly in the epic description — e.g. 'SC1 test: create PR2 with un-reviewed SHA on base==main; assert review-gate status=failure on GitHub Checks API'. This makes the test plan self-contained and auditable without chasing external docs.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT ('every commit reaching main must pass an LLM review that is ruleset-enforced'); SC3 ('live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review; the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'); SC4 ('a commit whose diff is entirely within the event-sourced ticket store is exempt')
findings.finding:
The plan makes at least three load-bearing environmental assertions that an executor must verify against the live repository before proceeding:

1. **Existing ruleset structure**: SC3 presupposes a live `main` ruleset already exists with a `required-checks` list that currently contains `merge-pipeline-checks` (to be swapped to `review-gate`). If the ruleset does not exist, or if the check name is different, the swap step is invalid. This is not confirmed in the plan text.

2. **`required-checks.txt` as source of truth**: The plan treats `required-checks.txt` as a file that mirrors the live ruleset. Whether this file exists, what its current contents are, and whether the R8 live-parity guard already reads it is unverified from this text.

3. **Event-sourced ticket store boundary**: SC4 exempts 'a commit whose diff is entirely within the event-sourced ticket store' from the review gate. The plan does not verify that a deterministic glob/path pattern already exists for the ticket store, nor that the current allowlist logic correctly captures it without false-negatives (e.g., a malicious actor naming a behavior-bearing file with a ticket-store path). Whether any allowlist implementation already exists in the repo is unconfirmed.

All three are AMBIGUOUS because they can only be resolved by inspecting the live repo, not the plan text alone.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a one-time 'prerequisites check' step (can be story 0 or a pre-flight gate in story 1) that explicitly verifies: (a) live ruleset exists and enumerates its current required-checks; (b) `required-checks.txt` exists and its content matches the live ruleset; (c) the ticket-store path glob is defined and tested for false-negative/positive exclusions. Document the expected state and the command used to verify (e.g., `gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets`).

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'); SC3 ('required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'); SC5 ('docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config … removed from the review-gate allowlist'); DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('the single enforce-flip … is hard-gated')
findings.finding:
The plan names several specific files that will be modified or created:
- `.github/workflows/review-coverage-invariant.yml`
- `.github/workflows/dangling-references.yml`
- `.github/workflows/ruleset-invariants.yml`
- `required-checks.txt`
- Allowlist configuration (path unspecified)
- ADR-0020 (`docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md`)

The following gaps affect scope accuracy:

1. **Allowlist file location not named**: SC5 says 'removed from the review-gate allowlist' but the actual file (or config block within a file) that holds the allowlist is never named. This is a missing edit target — the executor must guess or search.

2. **`review-gate` check implementation file absent**: The new `review-gate` required check is the central artifact of the entire epic. No workflow filename or script path is named for it. For a plan that names the three existing carrier YAMLs explicitly, this is a conspicuous omission.

3. **Blast radius of the enforce-flip on concurrent workflows not acknowledged**: Swapping `merge-pipeline-checks` → `review-gate` in the live ruleset will immediately affect every in-flight PR2 at the moment of swap. The plan notes 'land the swap with no other PR2 in flight — R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<>file skew window', which partially acknowledges this, but does not name the runbook document or the specific steps to drain in-flight PRs.

4. **Consumers of `required-checks.txt`**: If other scripts, dashboards, or CI steps read `required-checks.txt` they must also be updated. Whether such consumers exist is unverifiable from this text (AMBIGUOUS).
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Name the allowlist config file (e.g., `.github/review-gate-allowlist.txt` or a section in a YAML) in SC5. 2. Add the new `review-gate` workflow filename to the edit set. 3. In the enforce-flip story, reference the specific runbook doc (filename + section) and enumerate the drain-in-flight steps. 4. Add a grep/search step for consumers of `required-checks.txt` as part of the story-1 pre-flight.

findings.location: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified') vs. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Warn-first: every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode and is validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip') vs. SC3 ('the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset')
findings.finding:
Three internal tensions exist:

1. **Story 8 independence vs. SC6 requirement**: SEQUENCING says 'story 8 (UX) is independent and may land any time', but SC6 requires 'the two-tier flow is retained and its UX correctness fixes land' as an epic-close condition. If story 8 is truly independent (can land after epic close), there is no contradiction. But if SC6 is an epic-close gate and story 8 is the only story that delivers UX fixes, story 8 must complete before the epic can close — making it NOT truly independent of the story 7 → epic-close chain. The plan does not resolve this.

2. **Parallel stories 1–4 vs. warn-first validation sequencing**: The warn-first principle requires each story's warn-mode to be 'validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip.' If stories 1–4 are parallelised, their warn-mode validations may interleave, making it ambiguous which story's observable triggers a course-correction and which warn-validation results are prerequisites for story 5. The plan does not specify whether warn-mode validation of all four parallel stories must complete before story 5 begins, or only the declared code dependencies (1+2+4).

3. **SC3 'swap reflected on required-checks.txt AND live ruleset' vs. R8 live-parity guard**: SC3 requires the swap to be reflected in both artifacts simultaneously (or near-simultaneously). Yet the R8 live-parity guard is described as failing-closed 'under enforce mode' when drift exists — implying a window where the file and live ruleset can diverge. The plan does not specify the allowed drift window or the ordering of the two writes (file first vs. ruleset first), which means an executor could inadvertently trigger R8 RED during the swap itself.
findings.suggested_edit: 1. Clarify story 8's relationship to epic close: either add story 8 as a dependency of the epic-close condition in SC6 or explicitly state SC6's UX clause is satisfied by story 8 independently of the epic-close gate. 2. Add an explicit synchronisation point: 'warn-mode validation of stories 1–4 must each produce a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER=no-fire result before story 5 begins.' 3. In the enforce-flip runbook, specify the write order (file first or live ruleset first) and the maximum tolerated drift window before R8 fires.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA: SC1 ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'), SC2 ('Verified: a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED'), SC3 ('the R8 live-parity guard fail-closes under enforce so PAT expiry/scope-strip/drift cannot silently disable it'), SC4 ('coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs'), SC5 ('Verified: a docs-only PR now requires review; an image/lockfile PR still skips')
findings.finding: The majority of the success criteria explicitly require live GitHub interaction (live PR2 traffic, real ruleset checks, PAT token expiry/scope-stripping, real merge operations) and named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8). These cannot be completed within a single agent working session: they require real GitHub repository state, admin PAT provisioning, non-admin containment reads, and observable live CI outcomes. SC1 requires an 'allowlisted-only PR2' to actually be blocked at the GitHub level; SC2 requires token expiry/scope-stripping to be tested against live CI runners; SC3 references a live ruleset swap. None of these are simulatable from supplied auth alone — the plan acknowledges this explicitly ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub'). There is no offline/stub/mock path described for any of these verifications, making the criteria not completable in-session for an autonomous agent.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add a two-tier verification path: (a) a local/offline assertion (e.g., script exit-code tests with a stub token, a dry-run yaml lint + required-checks.txt diff) that an agent can run in-session, and (b) a clearly marked 'requires live GitHub validation' step that is explicitly the human operator's responsibility post-merge. This separates what the agent can verify from what requires a human live-ops step.

findings.location: Plan body ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel … story 5 depends on 1+2+4 … story 6 depends on 5 … story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent') vs. SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6)
findings.finding: The plan references 8 numbered stories with dependency relationships, but no story content is included in the plan text — only dependency sequencing is stated. SC1–SC6 map to epic-level outcomes, but there is no criterion that maps to individual story acceptance or that verifies story 8 (UX: 'descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle'). SC6 partially covers this ('flow preserved + documented') but does not name the UX story deliverables explicitly. Conversely, the experiments V0.1–V0.12 and E-series are referenced in SCs but not defined anywhere in the plan, making it impossible to confirm coverage without access to docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md. Terminology is mostly consistent, but 'merge-pipeline-checks' (SC3) appears only once and is never defined in the body, while 'review-gate' and 'llm-review' appear in multiple SCs without a cross-reference to where these checks are defined.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the story definitions (or at minimum their acceptance criteria) or add a mapping table: Story N → SC(s) it satisfies. Define 'merge-pipeline-checks' in a glossary or inline note. Add a criterion covering story 8 UX deliverables explicitly.

findings.location: Multiple locations: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)'), SC3 ('the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'), SC1 ('a single always-runs required check `review-gate` exists'), story dependency note ('story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified')
findings.finding: Multiple decision points are deferred or left underspecified for an executing agent: (1) The 8 stories are referenced by number and dependency but their content is entirely absent — an agent cannot act on 'story 5 depends on 1+2+4' without knowing what story 5 does. (2) 'admin PAT: provision/read' — the plan does not specify where the PAT comes from, what scopes it needs, or how it is injected into CI; an agent would have to stop and ask. (3) 'the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' is described only as an outcome in SC3 with no description of what files/configs change or how the swap is performed. (4) 'COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' is mentioned as a per-story artifact but no triggers are defined in the plan text — an agent cannot evaluate them. (5) SC4's 'event-sourced ticket store' exemption and SC5's allowlist are referenced but the allowlist contents and exemption logic are never stated. These are real design decisions pushed onto the executor.
findings.suggested_edit: For each of the 8 stories, provide at minimum: the files/configs to create or modify, the specific command or script to run, and the observable pass/fail signal. Define PAT scope requirements explicitly (e.g., `repo` + `admin:repo_hook`). Inline or reference the allowlist contents and the ticket-store path pattern for the exemption.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA preamble ('epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'), DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('story 7 (enforce-flip) is the only hard-to-reverse step and is hard-gated'), SC3 ('R8 live-parity guard fail-closes under enforce')
findings.finding: The plan defines a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the terminal condition but never describes what the completion-verifier is, what it checks, or where it runs. An agent cannot know when it is done. Additionally, SC3 references 'R8' (a named runbook/guard) and 'required-checks.txt' as source-of-truth artifacts, but neither is defined in the plan — without access to docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md the agent cannot verify parity. SC1's 'per-SHA coverage' ledger is mentioned as a verification mechanism but the ledger's location, format, and update protocol are unspecified. The enforce-flip in story 7 is described as 'land the swap with no other PR2 in flight — R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window' — this is a live operational constraint with no automated verification path. The gap between the steps described and the end-state (a fully enforced, live, durably configured ruleset) requires several live GitHub administrative actions that have no described completion signal beyond human observation.
findings.suggested_edit: Define the completion-verifier explicitly: name the script, its inputs, and the conditions under which it exits 0. Add a termination checklist (e.g., 'required-checks.txt matches live ruleset via `gh api` call', 'review-gate status check returns required=true on a test PR'). For the enforce-flip, add an automated post-swap assertion step.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT and opening paragraph
Title vs. plan body and SUCCESS CRITERIA
findings.finding: The plan body, delivery principles, and success criteria are all consistently oriented toward hardening the two-tier flow into a ruleset-enforced, fail-closed review gate, exactly as the title states. Story 8 (UX fixes) is a modest extension but is explicitly scoped as 'independent' and covered by SC6. The ADR reference and experiment chain (V0.1–V0.12) are referenced as prior art, not new scope. No contradictions or significant scope drift detected.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub'); SC1 'Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'; SC3 'live main ruleset requires review-gate … reflected on … live ruleset'; SC2 'a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED … in ALL THREE carriers'
findings.finding: The majority of the acceptance criteria are verifiable only against a live GitHub repository with real rulesets, a real admin PAT, real PR2 traffic, and real ruleset enforcement. SC1 requires a live GitHub PR2 to be BLOCKED; SC3 requires checking the live main ruleset and live required-checks.txt; SC2 requires a live CI run with a token stripped of scope; SC4 requires warm-hit ledger checks across re-runs of real CI. None of these can be simulated or completed within a single agent session without live GitHub access, real credentials, and live PR traffic. The 'warn-first → enforce-flip' sequencing also demands inter-story validation windows that span multiple sessions. These criteria are structurally out-of-session. The only partially in-session portions are file-editing tasks (YAML, docs, required-checks.txt), which are not individually broken out as independently verifiable acceptance criteria.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add a file-level or scriptable proxy assertion that an agent can actually execute (e.g., 'running ./scripts/test-gate.sh with a stub token exits non-zero', 'grep required-checks.txt shows review-gate present', 'unit test for coverage-ledger warm-hit passes'). Keep the live-GitHub validations as post-deploy verification steps outside the agent's acceptance criteria, or scope each story to exactly the artifacts the agent produces and treat live verification as a separate operations story.

findings.location: Plan body 'Phase 2 (§5)' and 'review-#4/#6 prerequisites'; SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6; SEQUENCING section; references to 'stories 1-4', 'story 5/6/7/8'
findings.finding:
1. Orphan references: The plan body repeatedly references 'Phase 2 (§5)', 'review-#4/#6 prerequisites', 'R8', 'E2/E5/E6'/E8/E11/E13/E14/E15/V0.3a-post-fix/V0.11/E-compute-hash' experiments, and 'stories 1-8' — but none of these stories, experiments, or phases are enumerated or described in this ticket. An agent has to fetch the external documents (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020) to understand what the work actually entails. The acceptance criteria essentially index into an external doc rather than being self-contained.
2. Described work has no criterion: The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES mention a per-story COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER and a runbook for the enforce-flip skew-window, but no success criterion verifies their existence or correctness.
3. Terminology inconsistency: 'merge-pipeline-checks' (SC3) vs. implied 'llm-review' check name vs. 'review-gate' — the relationship and rename path is mentioned only obliquely ('swap is reflected'). Also 'two-tier flow' vs. 'two-tier hardening' vs. 'fail-closed review gate' are used interchangeably for the same concept.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline the story list with their scope, or at minimum add a table mapping story# → artifact produced → SC it closes. Add an explicit SC or sub-criterion verifying that every story's COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER is documented. Resolve the 'merge-pipeline-checks → review-gate' rename inline (state exactly which string is the old name and which is the new name being required).

findings.location: Plan body: 'stories 1-8' (none listed); 'Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md'; SC5 'removed from the review-gate allowlist'; SC3 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap'; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'land the swap with no other PR2 in flight'
findings.finding:
Multiple execution-blocking ambiguities are present:
1. The actual work items (stories 1-8) are never enumerated in the plan; the agent must read an external document to know what to build. The plan says 'Do NOT use /dso:sprint' but provides no substitute decomposition inline.
2. SC5 refers to 'removed from the review-gate allowlist' without specifying what is currently on the allowlist or where it lives (file path, ruleset UI, YAML key?).
3. SC3's 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' is a critical rename/replacement action whose exact mechanics (which file, which ruleset API call, atomic vs. two-step) are deferred entirely to the external ADR.
4. The 'admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads' distinction is stated but no guidance is given on how an agent should handle credential provisioning if the PAT is absent.
5. The enforce-flip runbook is referenced as something a story 'carries' but its content is not specified here; an agent cannot verify completeness without the external plan.
6. SC4 mentions 'event-sourced ticket store' exemption without defining what path glob constitutes that store.
findings.suggested_edit: Enumerate stories 1-8 inline with at minimum: name, key artifact(s), and the allowlist/path patterns they touch. Specify the allowlist file path for SC5. For the enforce-flip, inline the critical steps (e.g., 'atomically update required-checks.txt AND ruleset in the same PR; no other PR2 in flight means: check via gh pr list --base main'). Define the ticket-store path glob for SC4's exemption.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA header ('epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS'); SEQUENCING ('story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified'); SC2/SC3 live verification steps
findings.finding: The plan names a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the final gate for epic closure, but the completion-verifier is never defined in this ticket (what script/workflow/check it is, where it lives, what it asserts). Without knowing what the completion-verifier checks, it is impossible to determine whether the union of described steps reaches the stated end-state. Additionally, SC2 and SC3 require live GitHub state (live ruleset, live CI runs) as terminal conditions; if those live states cannot be reached in-session or if the completion-verifier is an external tool, termination is structurally indeterminate for an agent. It cannot be assessed from the plan alone whether the described steps (file edits, YAML changes, required-checks.txt updates) plus live verification will provably close every SC — the external documents are needed to make that judgment.
findings.suggested_edit: Define the completion-verifier inline: what it is (a script? a workflow? a checklist?), where it lives, and exactly what assertions it makes against each SC.
