For each SC that requires a live check, name a concrete command or API call that constitutes 'verified' (e.g., 'gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets/{id} | jq .required_status_checks | grep review-gate exits 0').

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES; SUCCESS CRITERIA preamble
Title; NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SC1–SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
findings.finding: The plan body consistently pursues ruleset-enforced, fail-closed hardening of the two-tier flow as promised in the title. SC1 (single gate), SC2 (fail-closed), SC3 (live ruleset durability), SC4 (no false wedge), SC5 (surface coverage), SC6 (flow preserved + documented) all directly serve the stated goal. Story 8 (UX) is called out as independent but clearly scoped to the same flow. No described work contradicts the headline intent, and the scope does not visibly drift into unrelated areas. The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES (warn-first, reversibility) are supporting mechanisms that serve the same goal, not scope additions.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section + success criteria SC1-SC6
SC2: 'ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3: 'required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset'
findings.finding: The plan names three separate CI workflow files each independently enforcing fail-closed token/precondition behaviour, plus a parallel file-based snapshot (required-checks.txt) that must stay in sync with the live GitHub ruleset. Without access to the codebase it is impossible to confirm whether shared utilities already centralise this logic or whether it is being duplicated per-carrier. If the fail-closed wrapper logic is copy-pasted into each of the three YAMLs rather than extracted to a reusable composite action or shared script, that is gratuitous duplication. Similarly, required-checks.txt as a hand-maintained mirror of the live ruleset is a two-source-of-truth risk that may not be justified if the live ruleset is already the single source queried by the parity guard.
findings.suggested_edit: If the fail-closed exit logic is not already in a shared composite action, extract it to one (e.g. .github/actions/fail-closed-check/action.yml) and have all three carriers call it. Consider whether required-checks.txt is genuinely needed or whether the R8 parity guard can query the live ruleset directly, eliminating the manual sync step.

findings.location: SC1–SC5 + E/V-series experiment references throughout

SC1: 'a single always-runs required check `review-gate` exists'; SC3: 'live `main` ruleset requires `review-gate` and retains `llm-review`'; SEQUENCING: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start'
findings.finding: The plan asserts (1) that the check name `review-gate` does not already exist in the live ruleset and that `llm-review` is currently a required check that needs to be retained — both unverified against the live GitHub repository configuration. (2) It asserts the live ruleset state is such that the merge-pipeline-checks→review-gate swap is safe to execute — but if any existing required check happens to share the proposed name, or if `llm-review` has already been removed, the sequencing and gating logic breaks. (3) The claim that 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel' implicitly assumes no shared file or ruleset resource contention between them; this has not been verified for the actual workflow files in the repo.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a pre-execution checkpoint (could be a new story 0 or a prerequisite note) that scripts: (a) `gh api /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets` to snapshot current required checks; (b) confirms `review-gate` is absent and `llm-review` is present; (c) confirms no parallel stories touch the same YAML or ruleset resource. Document the expected output so the executor can verify before proceeding.

findings.location: SC2: 'review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC5: 'removed from the review-gate allowlist'; SC3: 'required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'
findings.finding: The plan names three specific workflow YAML files and references an allowlist and a required-checks.txt file, but does not enumerate: (a) any other callers/consumers of the current `merge-pipeline-checks` check name that would break when it is replaced by `review-gate`; (b) branch protection rules, CODEOWNERS entries, or other rulesets that might reference the old check name; (c) the specific allowlist file path and its current entries that will be edited under SC5. Without codebase access it cannot be confirmed these are the complete set of touch-points, but the omission is a plan-level gap — an executor might miss downstream consumers of the old check name.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a section listing: (1) the explicit file path of the allowlist (e.g. `.github/review-gate-allowlist.txt` or equivalent); (2) a grep/search step to find all references to `merge-pipeline-checks` (or whatever check is being renamed/replaced) across .github/ before executing the swap; (3) acknowledgement of any CODEOWNERS or additional rulesets that reference the old check name.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'Warn-first' vs NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SEQUENCING 'story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified'; SC2 fail-closed under 'enforce mode'
findings.finding: There is a potential internal tension: the NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT states every commit reaching `main` MUST pass an LLM review that is ruleset-enforced and fail-closed, yet the Warn-first delivery principle says every story ships in warn/advisory mode first with a deferred single enforce-flip. Under warn mode the gate does NOT block merges, so during stories 1–6 the non-negotiable requirement is technically not met. This is not necessarily wrong (it is a phased rollout pattern), but the plan does not explicitly acknowledge this temporary divergence or set a time-box on the warn-mode phase, which could allow the warn phase to persist indefinitely if the enforce-flip story (7) is deprioritised. Additionally, SC2 describes fail-closed behaviour under 'enforce mode' but the three carriers also run during warn mode — it is ambiguous whether fail-closed token behaviour is required in warn mode or only after the flip.
findings.suggested_edit: Add an explicit statement that the NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT applies to the post-flip steady state and that the warn-mode phase has a hard time-box (e.g. 'warn mode must not persist beyond N sprints without a documented extension decision'). Clarify in SC2 whether token/precondition fail-closed behaviour is required in warn mode (advisory exit-non-zero but not blocking) or only post-flip.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section and 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 (enforce-flip) depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 (UX) is independent'

SC2: 'fail-closed on precondition/token failure under enforce mode … ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml' and SC3: 'R8 live-parity guard fail-closes under enforce'
findings.finding: Three separate CI workflow files each independently re-implement the same fail-closed precondition/token-expiry guard logic. Without seeing the live codebase it is unclear whether a shared composite action or shell library already centralises this pattern, or whether three copies will grow divergently. If the guard logic is duplicated verbatim across the three carriers it violates the Rule-of-Three and creates a maintenance hazard where a future fix must be applied in three places. This cannot be confirmed from plan text alone.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a note: 'Extract the fail-closed precondition check into a shared composite action (e.g. .github/actions/fail-closed-guard) consumed by all three carriers, so the guard logic has a single point of truth.'

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC6) and 'DELIVERY PRINCIPLES: Warn-first … Live-verify each story on real GitHub … named E-series/V-series experiments'
findings.finding: The plan names specific experiment references (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) tied to observable outcomes for each success criterion. Failure-path coverage is explicitly called out (SC2: scope-stripped/expired token makes gate RED; SC4: no false wedge on clean PR; SC1: allowlisted-only PR carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED). RED-before-GREEN ordering is encoded via the warn-first delivery principle with a hard enforce-flip gate as the final step. The course-correction trigger mechanism provides boundary-case coverage at the story level.

findings.location: SC3: 'live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'; SC1: 'a single always-runs required check review-gate exists … no code_changed skip'; SC4: 'a commit whose diff is entirely within the event-sourced ticket store is exempt'
findings.finding: Three significant environmental premises are asserted without verification steps named in the plan: (1) The live GitHub ruleset currently has a specific shape (retains 'llm-review', currently requires 'merge-pipeline-checks') — if this has already drifted, the swap logic in SC3 is wrong from the start. (2) The 'no code_changed skip' claim about 'review-gate' assumes the check does not inherit a path-filter from an existing reusable workflow definition that is not shown. (3) The ticket-store exemption logic in SC4 assumes the exemption boundary (i.e. what exactly constitutes 'event-sourced ticket store') is already precisely defined somewhere (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md is referenced but not quoted). None of these are verified with an explicit 'read live state before proceeding' step in the plan.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a pre-flight verification step at the start of the epic (or Story 1): (a) snapshot the live ruleset required-checks list and compare to required-checks.txt; (b) confirm the 'review-gate' check definition has no inherited path-filter; (c) record the exact glob pattern that defines the ticket-store exemption boundary in the epic body.

findings.location: SC2: 'review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3: 'required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'; SC5: 'docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config … removed from the review-gate allowlist'
findings.finding: The plan names three workflow files and two config artefacts (required-checks.txt, the allowlist) but does not enumerate the full edit-set: (a) The allowlist file itself is never named (its path is not given — only that docs/** and skills will be removed from it). (b) 'behavior-driving config' is undefined — it is unclear which files this refers to and therefore which callers/consumers of the allowlist may need updating. (c) The 'merge-pipeline-checks -> review-gate swap' in SC3 implies a rename or replacement of a CI job, but no workflow file where the old job lives is identified. Without the live codebase these cannot be confirmed wrong, but the omissions would leave an executor guessing at scope.
findings.suggested_edit: Name the allowlist file explicitly (e.g. '.github/review-gate-allowlist.txt' or wherever it lives). Define 'behavior-driving config' with a concrete glob or file list. Name the workflow file that currently contains 'merge-pipeline-checks' so the swap target is unambiguous.

findings.location: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel') vs. SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers fail-closed') vs. DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('warn-first: every story ships in warn mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip')
findings.finding: Two potential tensions exist between sections, though neither is a clear contradiction: (1) The plan says stories 1-4 may run in parallel, but SC2 requires all three carriers to be fail-closed simultaneously before the epic closes. If different stories own different carriers (which is implied but not stated explicitly), a parallelism bug could allow story 7 (enforce-flip) to proceed while one carrier is still in warn mode. The dependency graph does not explicitly map 'carrier X fail-closed' to a specific story number, so an executor cannot verify the dependency is complete. (2) SC4 ('coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs — no flaky-check team block') is a correctness property that could conflict with SC1 ('no code_changed skip') — an always-runs check that hits a cold cache on first run could still cause transient failures even if eventually warm. The plan does not describe how cache initialisation is handled on the first run after deploy. These are tensions that could cause rework rather than hard contradictions.
findings.suggested_edit: In the SEQUENCING section, add a mapping: 'Story N owns carrier review-coverage-invariant.yml, Story M owns dangling-references.yml, Story P owns ruleset-invariants.yml — all three must be in enforce mode before Story 7 gate.' Also add a note in SC4 about cache warm-up strategy for the first post-deploy run.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub'); NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT
findings.finding:
Multiple acceptance criteria require out-of-session, live-GitHub operations that cannot be completed or verified by an agent in a single working session without persistent access to a real GitHub environment:

1. SC1: 'Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED (not skip-to-success)' — requires a live PR2 to exist in the repo, a real GitHub ruleset to be active, and an observable block event.
2. SC2: 'Verified: a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED' in ALL THREE carriers — requires deliberate token manipulation and observation of live CI runs.
3. SC3: 'live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review' and 'the R8 live-parity guard fail-closes under enforce so PAT expiry/scope-strip/drift cannot silently disable it' — requires reading and confirming live GitHub ruleset configuration.
4. SC4: 'the coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs (no flaky-check team block)' — requires multiple CI re-runs observed over time.
5. Delivery Principle 'Warn-first': 'validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip' — implies waiting for real PR traffic to accumulate.
6. Delivery Principle 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)' — named as a blanket requirement across all stories.

None of these verifications can be simulated or stubbed within a self-contained agent session. The plan contains no fallback measurability path (e.g., a mock GitHub API, a local ruleset-emulation, or recorded fixtures) for any of these criteria. This means the agent cannot close SC1–SC3 or validate the enforce-flip gate (story 7) without a live persistent environment and real PR traffic.
findings.suggested_edit: For each live-verification criterion, add a two-track measurability clause: (a) the live-GitHub verification that a human operator performs, and (b) an agent-executable proxy (e.g., 'the GitHub API returns required_status_checks including review-gate' via a recorded fixture or a dry-run script that can be run in CI against a sandbox repo). Mark the live-verification track as 'human-signed-off' and the agent track as 'automated-pass'. This separates what an agent can complete from what requires human observation.

findings.location: Plan description; NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SUCCESS CRITERIA preamble
SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC5; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES; 'named E-series/V-series experiments'
findings.finding:
The plan references a rich set of named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13–E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) and explicitly ties each SC to one or more of them. This is a positive signal. However, the plan itself does not describe what those experiments actually do, what their pass/fail oracles are, or where they are defined — they are purely referenced by opaque identifiers. Without access to docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md or docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md (the 'source of truth'), it is impossible to confirm:
- Whether failure-path coverage exists (e.g., token expiry → RED, not exit-0) or is only implied by the experiment labels.
- Whether RED-before-GREEN ordering is enforced in any of these experiments.
- Whether boundary cases like 'diff entirely within the event-sourced ticket store' (SC4 exemption) are tested with a negative case (non-exempt content mistakenly placed in the store).
- Whether the warn-mode → enforce-mode transition itself is tested (i.e., that warn-mode does NOT block, and enforce-mode DOES).

The plan also does not describe how story-level tests are structured (unit, integration, or only CI-live), making it impossible to assess coverage depth from the ticket alone.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline a one-line description of each named experiment in the ticket (or in a linked table) specifying: (1) what it does, (2) its pass oracle, and (3) its failure/boundary oracle. At minimum, confirm that at least one experiment covers the RED-before-GREEN transition for the enforce-flip (story 7) and the token-expiry fail-closed path (SC2).

findings.location: Multiple sections
findings.finding:
Several coherence and terminology issues exist:

1. **Orphan mechanism in description with no SC owner**: The 'COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' named in the Delivery Principles is described as a per-story observable, but no SC verifies that these triggers are defined and documented for each story. If a trigger is missing or wrong, the plan has no criterion to catch it.

2. **Terminology inconsistency — 'merge-pipeline-checks' vs. 'review-gate'**: SC3 references 'the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' as a named action, but the description and NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT use 'review-gate' exclusively without establishing what 'merge-pipeline-checks' is or was. This risks ambiguity about what is being swapped from/to.

3. **Terminology inconsistency — 'R8'**: SC2 ('R8 fail-closed'), SC3 ('R8 live-parity guard'), and the Delivery Principles ('R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window') all reference 'R8' but it is never defined in the ticket. It appears to refer to a specific runbook or ruleset check, but the reader cannot tell.

4. **Near-duplicate coverage between SC2 and SC3**: SC2 requires fail-closed behavior on token/precondition failure in all three carriers; SC3 requires that 'the R8 live-parity guard fail-closes under enforce so PAT expiry/scope-strip/drift cannot silently disable it.' The PAT-expiry/scope-strip condition in SC3 is a subset of SC2's coverage and is not distinguished in terms of which carrier or which code path. This creates potential double-counting without clarity on whether SC3 adds a genuinely new gate or restates SC2.

5. **Described work with no SC**: The 'Reversibility' delivery principle (the enforce-flip runbook, the 'no other PR2 in flight' requirement, the R8 RED window) is described in the plan body but has no corresponding acceptance criterion to verify it was executed correctly.

6. **'completion-verifier PASS' in success preamble is undefined**: 'epic closes only when ALL are met + completion-verifier PASS' — the completion-verifier is never described, linked, or defined in the ticket.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Add an SC (or extend an existing one) to explicitly verify that each story's COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER is documented and observable.
2. Define 'merge-pipeline-checks' and 'R8' inline or in a glossary section.
3. Clarify whether SC3's PAT-expiry clause is testing the same path as SC2 or a distinct integration layer — if distinct, name the specific carrier/check; if duplicate, merge into SC2.
4. Either add an SC for the enforce-flip reversibility runbook (was it executed per the documented steps?) or explicitly mark it as an operational procedure outside of SC scope.
5. Define or link the 'completion-verifier' artifact.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub'); NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria explicitly require live GitHub operations that cannot be completed by an agent in a single working session without real external state: SC1 requires a live PR2 to be BLOCKED by a real GitHub ruleset ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'); SC2 requires a scope-stripped/expired token to make a gate RED across three real CI carriers; SC3 requires the live `main` ruleset to reflect a check-swap and a live R8 parity guard to fail-close on PAT expiry; SC6 requires the enforce-flip to land with 'no other PR2 in flight'. These are not simulatable assertions — they require real GitHub API state, real CI runs on real PRs, real ruleset enforcement, and potentially coordination windows. There is no stated simulation substitute or mocked equivalent for any of these. Additionally, the sequencing note ('story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified') implies multi-session ordering that an agent cannot self-satisfy. SC4's 'no flaky-check team block' is an observational criterion over time (coverage ledger 'warm-hits across re-runs'), not a single-run assertion. Unmeasurable within session: SC6's 'UX correctness fixes land' and 'docs reflect the final enforced architecture' lack a concrete checkable artifact definition.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC that requires live GitHub verification, add an explicit in-session proxy: e.g., 'verified via recorded API response fixture / dry-run script output against a test repo with admin PAT' or 'verified by asserting the ruleset JSON payload equals the expected schema in a unit test.' Where live-only verification is truly required, mark those SCs as post-deploy acceptance checks and separate them from in-session agent-completable criteria.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; plan description paragraph 1; ADR-0020 reference
SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES; experiment references V0.1–V0.12, E2/E5/E6'/E8/E11/E13/E14/E15/E-compute-hash
findings.finding: The plan names many experiments (V0.1–V0.12, E-series) as verification evidence but does not describe HOW each experiment is structured, what its RED-before-GREEN ordering is, or what its pass/fail assertion looks like. As written the experiments are opaque references: a reader (or agent) cannot reconstruct what 'E2/E13' tests, what input triggers a RED state for V0.3a-post-fix, or what 'R8 fail-closed' asserts. Failure-path coverage is named (token expiry, scope-strip, precondition failure, allowlisted-only PR with un-reviewed content) but the mechanism to induce and assert each failure is absent. Boundary cases for the allowlist (SC5: 'behavior-bearing surfaces removed from allowlist') have no defined test matrix: which file globs are in/out, and how the check is asserted to correctly classify a mixed PR. The coverage-ledger warm-hit test (SC4) is described as an outcome to observe, not as a repeatable test step. There is no RED-before-GREEN statement for any of the CI check changes, and no description of how the warn→enforce mode flip is tested before it is activated.
findings.suggested_edit: For each named experiment/V-series item, add a two-line test description: (1) input state/trigger, (2) expected assertion. Example: 'E2: create a PR with one un-reviewed SHA targeting main → assert review-gate exits non-zero and GitHub check is RED; E13: same PR with allowlist-only diff → assert review-gate exits 0.' Add a failure-mode test table: token-expired → expected exit code and output; precondition-failure → expected exit code. Define the allowlist boundary matrix for SC5. State RED-before-GREEN for each CI change (e.g., 'land warn mode, observe advisory failure on a known-bad PR, then flip to enforce').

findings.location: SC2 vs SC3 ('R8 fail-closed' / 'R8 live-parity guard'); SC4 ('coverage ledger warm-hits'); SC5 ('event-sourced ticket store'); DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('warn-first') vs SC1 ('single always-runs required check')
findings.finding: Several terminology inconsistencies and orphan/ambiguous references exist: (1) 'R8' appears in SC2 ('R8 fail-closed'), SC3 ('R8 live-parity guard'), and the DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('R8 RED is repo-wide') without being defined anywhere in the plan body — it is unclear whether R8 is a story number, a ruleset name, or a CI job. (2) 'coverage ledger' (SC4) is referenced without definition or pointer to how it is implemented or where it lives. (3) 'event-sourced ticket store' (SC4/SC5) appears only in SC4/SC5 with no description in the plan body of what it is or how exemption is implemented. (4) 'merge-pipeline-checks→review-gate swap' (SC3) is described as reflected in 'required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset' — required-checks.txt is not introduced elsewhere; it is unclear if this is a file in the repo or an artifact of another process. (5) SC1 says 'single always-runs required check' while DELIVERY PRINCIPLES says 'warn-first … validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip' — the warn phase is advisory (not a hard gate), which is in tension with SC1's 'single always-runs required check' framing; the plan does not clarify that SC1 is the end-state and warn mode is transient. (6) The described work references 'Phase 2 (§5)' from the source-of-truth document but no section of the plan body elaborates what Phase 2 contains, leaving a potential orphan: work may be described in the external doc but have no corresponding AC here.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a terminology glossary or inline definitions for: R8 (is it Story 8? a ruleset name?), coverage ledger (file path + schema), event-sourced ticket store (what it is and where), required-checks.txt (repo path and owner). Clarify the warn→enforce lifecycle in SC1 by adding '(in enforce mode, the final state)' to avoid ambiguity with the warn-first delivery principle. Either inline a summary of Phase 2 §5 scope or add an AC that explicitly maps each Phase 2 §5 item to a named SC.

findings.location: Multiple sections: 'Source of truth: docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md', 'docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md', 'SEQUENCING: stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4...'
findings.finding: The plan body references 'stories 1-8' extensively (dependencies, sequencing, named experiments V0.x/E-series/R-series, per-SHA coverage, 'coverage ledger warm-hits', 'review-gate allowlist', 'live<->file skew window') but NONE of the individual story content is included in the ticket. An executing agent must stop to retrieve docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md, and presumably a story-breakdown document to understand what any individual deliverable actually requires. The plan is essentially a table-of-contents pointing entirely outside itself. Key design decisions deferred to external documents include: what 'per-SHA coverage' means mechanically, what the 'coverage ledger' is and how warm-hit caching works, what the 'enforce-flip runbook' contains, and what the 'completion-verifier' checks. Without access to those docs, an agent cannot proceed.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the story-level acceptance criteria and key design decisions into this ticket, or break this into separate tickets (one per story) each self-contained. At minimum, define 'per-SHA coverage', 'coverage ledger', and the enforce-flip runbook steps directly in this plan body.

findings.location: Title vs. SC1–SC6, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT

SC2: 'all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed... in ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3: 'live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'; SC4: 'a commit whose diff is entirely within the event-sourced ticket store is exempt'; SC5: 'behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config) are removed from the review-gate allowlist'
findings.finding: The plan makes several assertions about the existing environment that an executor must verify before relying on them: (1) That review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, and ruleset-invariants.yml already exist as CI workflow files and already contain token/precondition-check logic that can be made fail-closed — if any of these don't exist yet, the scope is dramatically different. (2) That a 'live main ruleset' already exists and currently requires 'llm-review' as a required check — if the ruleset is absent or structured differently, SC3 cannot be executed as written. (3) That an 'event-sourced ticket store' already exists with a stable, machine-readable boundary that can be used for exemption logic. (4) That a 'review-gate allowlist' already exists that currently includes docs/** and skills paths. All of these are presented as current facts, but cannot be confirmed without codebase access.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Preconditions / environment assumptions' section to the plan that explicitly lists each of the above as a verified prerequisite (with the file paths or API calls confirming them), or flag them as 'executor must verify before starting Story N'.

findings.location: SC1: 'a single always-runs required check review-gate'; SC2: 'ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'; SC3: 'required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset'; SC4: 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs'
findings.finding: Two potential over-engineering signals are present in the plan text, though confirmation requires codebase access: (1) Three separate CI workflow files each independently implement fail-closed token/precondition wrapper logic (SC2). If the fail-closed guard is non-trivial, duplicating it across three workflows rather than extracting a shared composite action or reusable workflow is an NIH/Rule-of-Three violation. (2) The 'coverage ledger' with cross-run warm-hit caching is introduced as an artifact of this epic. If GitHub's native required-status-checks mechanism already prevents merging on RED, a bespoke ledger that caches coverage across re-runs may be redundant complexity. Neither can be confirmed without seeing the existing workflow files and the ledger implementation.
findings.suggested_edit: If the fail-closed token-check logic is more than ~5 lines, extract it to a composite GitHub Action shared by all three workflows rather than duplicating it. Before building the coverage ledger's warm-hit cache, confirm that GitHub's native check-reuse (same SHA, same context name) does not already satisfy the no-flaky-check requirement (SC4).

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA section (SC1–SC6) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub')
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria require live GitHub operations that cannot be completed within a single agent session: SC1 requires observing a live PR2 being BLOCKED ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'); SC2 requires running the gate with a 'scope-stripped/expired token' against all three live CI carriers; SC3 requires the 'live `main` ruleset' to reflect a swap; SC4 requires warm-hitting a coverage ledger 'across re-runs'. None of these can be simulated with static auth or file-system assertions alone — they all presuppose real GitHub API state and actual CI pipeline runs. The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES reinforce this: 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub (admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads)'. Additionally, several verification references (E2, E13, V0.3a-post-fix, R8, E6', V0.11, etc.) point to experiment/run artefacts that are external to the repository and cannot be inspected or re-run by an agent in-session. There is no stated fallback (mock, stub, fixture, or recorded-replay) for any of these live-only checks.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC that requires live GitHub state, add an explicit in-session proxy: e.g., 'If running without live GitHub access, verified via: (a) a recorded API response fixture in tests/fixtures/github/, (b) a local act/nektos dry-run, or (c) a unit test that mocks the GitHub checks API and asserts the expected HTTP calls.' Mark any SC that is definitionally live-only (e.g., SC3 live ruleset parity) as a manual post-deploy gate, separate from the automated acceptance criteria that the agent is expected to close.

findings.location: Plan description opening paragraphs and NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT section
SUCCESS CRITERIA (SC1–SC5) and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story…by the named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The plan references an extensive experiment/verification suite (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8, etc.) that collectively provide behavioral, failure-path, and boundary coverage. However, these experiments are defined externally in docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md — the plan under review never reproduces their RED-before-GREEN ordering, boundary inputs, or failure-path triggers. It is therefore impossible to verify from this plan alone whether: (a) failure paths such as token expiry and scope-strip are tested RED-first before the fix is applied, (b) the coverage-ledger warm-hit test (SC4) uses a deterministic input rather than timing-dependent cache state, or (c) the 'no false wedge' boundary (SC4) for the event-sourced ticket store exemption is checked by a specific test rather than manual inspection. Without visibility into the referenced experiment definitions, completeness cannot be confirmed.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the relevant experiment definitions (inputs, expected outputs, RED step, GREEN step) for each SC-referenced experiment, or add a section 'Test plan summary' that tabulates: experiment id | what it tests | failure-path covered | how RED is established | pass signal. This makes the test plan self-contained and reviewable without the external docs.

findings.location: Multiple locations across SUCCESS CRITERIA and SEQUENCING sections
findings.finding:
Three coherence and terminology issues found:

1. ORPHAN CRITERIA — SC6 mentions 'UX correctness fixes land (descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle)' and references story 8, but the plan body (DELIVERY PRINCIPLES, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT) never describes these UX fixes; there is no corresponding description of what 'descriptive PR1 titles' means or what 'sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' entails. Story 8 is called out as independent with 'may land any time' but has no acceptance sub-criteria of its own.

2. TERMINOLOGY INCONSISTENCY — The gate is called 'review-gate' in SC1 and SC3 but the plan body also mentions 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' (SC3) and 'llm-review' as a separate required check. It is ambiguous whether 'llm-review' and 'review-gate' are two distinct required checks on the ruleset, or whether 'review-gate' replaces 'llm-review'. SC3 says the ruleset 'requires `review-gate` and retains `llm-review`' (two distinct checks), while SC1 refers to 'a single always-runs required check `review-gate`' — creating an apparent contradiction about whether one or two checks must be required.

3. DESCRIBED WORK WITH NO CRITERION — The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES describe a 'COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER' for each story, but no success criterion verifies that these triggers are documented or tested. This is described work (course-correction runbooks) with no matching AC.
findings.suggested_edit:
1. Add an inline description of the UX story (story 8) with its own measurable AC (e.g., 'PR1 title template applied and visible in GitHub UI; sprint-umbrella PR lifecycle documented in runbook').
2. Clarify in the description and SC3 whether 'llm-review' is a transitional check that is eventually replaced by 'review-gate', or a permanently co-required check. If transitional, add a criterion like 'SC3b: after the enforce-flip, `llm-review` is removed from required-checks.txt and the live ruleset; only `review-gate` remains required.'
3. Either add a criterion that course-correction triggers are documented and machine-readable (e.g., in a runbook file), or remove them from the plan body to avoid implying deliverable scope.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA section (SC1–SC6) and SEQUENCING paragraph
findings.finding: The plan names a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the final gate that closes the epic, but no definition of what the completion-verifier is, who runs it, how it is invoked, or what its PASS/FAIL surface looks like is given in this plan. The named experiments (E2, E5, E6′, E8, E11, E13–E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) are referenced as verification evidence for each SC but the plan does not describe how those experiment results are recorded or how reviewers/agents confirm each SC is satisfied. Without codebase access (to docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md and the ADR) it is impossible to confirm whether those documents fully specify the verifier and experiment procedures, so the verdict cannot be resolved to a clean PASS.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a brief 'Verification protocol' subsection that: (a) defines the completion-verifier (script name, invocation, expected output), (b) maps each SC to the specific CLI command or GitHub UI step that confirms it, and (c) states where experiment results are persisted (e.g., a file in docs/handoff/results/).

findings.location: SEQUENCING paragraph and 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel' description
NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SC2, SC3, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
findings.finding: The plan names several concrete artifacts — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml, required-checks.txt, and the live `main` ruleset — but does NOT enumerate: (1) which files/symbols must be created vs. modified, (2) which callers/consumers of the existing `llm-review` check reference it by name and would need updating when it is replaced by `review-gate` (e.g., other workflow files that depend_on or status-check that name), (3) what the 'merge-pipeline-checks -> review-gate swap' touches beyond required-checks.txt. The blast radius of renaming/replacing a required check is non-trivial (branch protection rules, external dashboards, README badges, downstream scripts) and is not acknowledged. Because the live codebase is unavailable, the full set of affected files cannot be confirmed.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Files touched / blast-radius' table listing: each .yml workflow being created or modified; the ruleset JSON/API call payload; required-checks.txt before/after diff; any README/docs that embed the check name; and a grep-search note for all occurrences of 'llm-review' and 'merge-pipeline-checks' that must be updated in lockstep with the swap.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('warn-first' + enforce-flip); SC3 ('retains llm-review'); NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT ('every commit reaching main must pass an LLM review … ruleset-enforced'); SC6 ('two-tier flow is retained')
findings.finding:
Two potential internal tensions exist that cannot be fully resolved without the referenced source documents:
1. SC3 says the live ruleset 'retains llm-review' after the swap, while the NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT says `review-gate` becomes the single required check. It is unclear whether 'retains llm-review' means the check continues to run (advisory) but is NOT a required ruleset check, or whether both checks remain required. If both remain required the plan contradicts SC1 ('a single always-runs required check').
2. The warn-first principle says 'every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode', but Story 7 (the enforce-flip) is described as 'the only hard-to-reverse step'. If earlier stories' warn-mode outputs are already wired into the ruleset as required checks, flipping them would be trivially reversible; if they are not in the ruleset, SC2/SC3's 'live ruleset' wording is premature. The relationship between advisory-mode operation and 'live ruleset' language needs clarifying.
findings.suggested_edit: Clarify in SC3: specify whether `llm-review` remains a ruleset-required check or is demoted to advisory-only after the swap. Add one sentence to Story 7 (enforce-flip) making explicit which checks change from advisory to required in that single flip, reconciling it with SC1's 'single required check' language.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA section (SC1–SC6) and SEQUENCING paragraph
findings.finding: The plan names a 'completion-verifier PASS' as the final gate for epic closure, but never describes what the completion-verifier is, where it lives, who runs it, or what its pass/fail conditions are. Each story is said to name a COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER, but those triggers are declared at the story level (not shown here), so it is impossible to verify from the epic plan alone that every story is covered. The end-state for SC3 references 'required-checks.txt' and 'live ruleset' parity, but no step in the plan describes how the live snapshot is captured or diffed against the file — that mechanism is implied by V0.11 but is not stated. Without seeing the story-level plans, it is ambiguous whether a complete verification path exists.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a brief 'Definition of Done / Completion Verifier' subsection to the epic plan that (a) names the script/workflow that is the completion-verifier, (b) lists the specific checks it runs (e.g., SC1 live-block test, SC2 token-strip smoke test, SC3 parity diff), and (c) states who triggers it (CI, maintainer, automated). Also cross-reference which story-level artifact produces the required-checks.txt snapshot used in SC3.

findings.location: SEQUENCING paragraph; SUCCESS CRITERIA section
SUCCESS CRITERIA SC2, SC3, SC5; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES (warn-first / enforce-flip)
findings.finding: The plan references concrete file/workflow names (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml, required-checks.txt, the live `main` ruleset, the coverage ledger, the 'review-gate' and 'llm-review' checks, merge-pipeline-checks) but never enumerates which story touches which file, nor which callers/consumers of the replaced 'merge-pipeline-checks' check must be updated (e.g., branch-protection rules, other workflow 'needs:' chains, status-check references in other YAMLs). The 'enforce-flip' in story 7 swaps a required check repo-wide, which is high blast-radius, and while the runbook mention acknowledges the live<->file skew window, no consumer audit is described. This is borderline acceptable at epic level, but the absence of even a 'files known to change' list means any child-story author lacks a safe starting boundary.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Known files / symbols touched' appendix to the epic plan listing at minimum: the three workflow YAMLs (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml), required-checks.txt, the ruleset API call target, and any workflow that currently declares 'merge-pipeline-checks' as a dependency or status check. Mark blast-radius as 'repo-wide required-check swap' and note that a grep for 'merge-pipeline-checks' across .github/** must be run before story 7.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES (warn-first) vs. SC2 (fail-closed under enforce mode); SEQUENCING (stories 1-4 parallel) vs. NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT
findings.finding: Two tensions worth flagging: (1) The warn-first principle states enforcing logic ships in warn/advisory mode and is 'validated on live PR2 traffic BEFORE any enforce-flip', but SC2 requires all three CI wrappers to fail-closed on token failure 'under enforce mode'. If the wrappers are in warn mode during stories 1–6, SC2 cannot be verified as written until story 7 — yet story 7 depends on 1–6 ALL verified including SC2. This creates a circular dependency: SC2 requires enforce mode, enforce mode requires story 7, story 7 requires SC2. The plan does not resolve this. (2) The SEQUENCING paragraph says stories 1–4 may proceed in parallel, but SC5 (behavior-bearing surfaces removed from allowlist) depends on story 4 and the plan states story 5 depends on 1+2+4 — it is unclear whether story 3 has any prerequisite from the parallel group, which could allow a story 3 merge to conflict with a story 4 allowlist change mid-flight.
findings.suggested_edit: For tension (1): clarify that SC2 is verified in a non-production test harness (e.g., a branch or staging environment) during the warn-mode stories, so the circular dependency is resolved before the enforce-flip. For tension (2): explicitly state story 3's dependencies (or confirm it is fully independent of 4) in the SEQUENCING paragraph.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section; individual story dependency clauses ('story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent')
findings.finding: The plan references 'each story' and 'story 1-8' throughout (dependencies, course-correction triggers, enforce-flip runbook, etc.) but the actual story definitions are NOT included in the plan text. An executing agent is told to 'see each story's dependency links' but those links/stories are absent from the reviewed artifact. The agent would have to stop and locate external story definitions before proceeding. Additionally, the plan says 'the enforce-flip story carries the runbook (land the swap with no other PR2 in flight — R8 RED is repo-wide during the live<->file skew window)' without specifying how the agent should verify that no other PR2 is in flight, nor exactly what 'R8 RED' means operationally. SC3 references 'the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap' without defining which specific GitHub required-check names are being swapped from/to. SC2 says 'ALL THREE carriers' are named but doesn't give the exact pass/fail logic each must implement.
findings.suggested_edit: Inline or explicitly attach the 8 story definitions (or reference a specific section/file that is in scope for the agent to read). For the enforce-flip story, define the 'no other PR2 in flight' pre-check as a concrete shell or API command. Spell out the old required-check name being replaced by 'review-gate'.

findings.location: Plan title vs. body/success criteria

SC1 ('no code_changed skip'), SC2 ('all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed'), SC3 ('live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'), SC4 ('coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs'), SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces removed from the review-gate allowlist')
findings.finding: Multiple success criteria rest on unverified assertions about the current state of the live codebase and GitHub configuration that an executor must confirm before acting: (1) SC1 assumes a 'code_changed' skip path currently exists in CI that must be removed — but whether this skip logic exists and where it lives is not confirmed in the plan text. (2) SC2 asserts there are exactly three CI carriers (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) that wrap enforcing logic — the plan does not confirm these files exist with the described structure. (3) SC3 assumes a live 'main' ruleset already exists on GitHub with 'llm-review' as a required check. (4) SC4 assumes a 'coverage ledger' caching mechanism exists. (5) SC5 assumes a 'review-gate allowlist' file already exists and that behavior-bearing surfaces are currently on it. Any of these could be wrong, causing the executor to build on a false premise.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a short 'Current-state assumptions' section that explicitly lists each assumed artifact (file paths, GitHub ruleset name, required-check names, allowlist file path) alongside the command or API call that verifies the assumption is true before the story begins. Mark each story's pre-conditions with 'VERIFY BEFORE START: <command>'.

findings.location: SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'); SC3 ('live<->file skew window', 'R8 live-parity guard'); SC4 ('coverage ledger warm-hits'); SC5 (allowlist + hash mechanism)
findings.finding: The plan layers multiple interlocking enforcement mechanisms (a ruleset required-check, three separate CI yml wrappers each independently fail-closed, a coverage ledger with warm-cache semantics, a live-parity guard (R8), an allowlist, and a compute-hash experiment). Without access to the codebase it cannot be confirmed whether simpler existing GitHub mechanisms (e.g., a single composite action, an existing CODEOWNERS + required-review count, or GitHub's native branch-protection 'Require review from Code Owners') could satisfy the NON-NEGOTIABLE requirement with far less machinery. If none of these already exist, the complexity may be justified; but the plan does not explain why each of the three CI carriers independently needs its own fail-closed token check rather than sharing a single wrapper, which risks duplicated error-handling logic (Rule-of-Three concern).
findings.suggested_edit: Add a one-paragraph 'Why not simpler?' rationale in the plan (or reference the ADR-0020 section that already provides it) explaining why native GitHub required-reviews or a single composite action cannot satisfy SC1/SC2, and why three separate yml carriers need independent token checks rather than a shared helper action.

findings.location: Multiple sections: SEQUENCING ('stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel after start; story 5 depends on 1+2+4; story 6 depends on 5; story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified; story 8 is independent'), SC1-SC6, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
findings.finding: The epic-level plan references 8 stories (Story 1–8) but never defines them inline. The plan says 'this epic translates plan Phase 2 (§5) + the review-#4/#6 prerequisites into shippable phases' and defers all story-level detail to external documents (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md). An executing agent cannot act on dependency sequencing (e.g. 'story 5 depends on 1+2+4'), story-specific COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGERs, or named experiments (E-series/V-series) without access to those external sources of truth. If those documents are not co-located in the ticket, the executor must stop and locate them. Additionally, several implementation details are referenced but not specified in this plan: (a) what exactly constitutes 'per-SHA coverage' and the coverage ledger schema; (b) what 'allowlist' in SC1/SC5 currently contains and what changes are required; (c) what the 'enforce-flip' swap concretely entails (which YAML keys change, in which files); (d) the runbook referenced for the 'live<->file skew window' is named but not included. These are real design decisions deferred to external documents rather than resolved here.
findings.suggested_edit: Either (a) inline the story definitions (story scope, acceptance criteria, COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER) directly into this ticket, or (b) add a prose summary of each story's concrete deliverable + the current state of the allowlist/coverage-ledger schema so an executor can proceed without fetching external docs. At minimum, enumerate what the enforce-flip swap changes (file paths and YAML keys) so Story 7's scope is unambiguous.

findings.location: Title, NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6

SC2 ('all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed… in ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'), SC3 ('live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'), SC4 ('the coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs'), SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces… are removed from the review-gate allowlist')
findings.finding:
The plan makes several structural assertions about the current codebase state that an executor must verify before relying on them, and that cannot be confirmed from the plan text alone:
1. It asserts that the three named YAML workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) already exist and already contain 'review-enforcing CI wrapper' logic that can be toggled — this may or may not reflect their actual current content.
2. It asserts the live main ruleset currently 'retains llm-review' as a required check — if the live ruleset has drifted this is a blocking gap.
3. It asserts a 'coverage ledger' exists and already warm-hits across re-runs — if this infrastructure doesn't yet exist, it is a build task, not a verification task.
4. It asserts the 'review-gate allowlist' currently contains behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config) that need removal — the current allowlist contents are unverified from the plan.
5. SC1 references 'origin/main..head per-SHA coverage' as if a known algorithm exists — the implementation of SHA coverage tracking is not specified.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'precondition checklist' section that enumerates: (a) which YAML files exist and their current warn/enforce toggle state; (b) the live ruleset's current required-checks list (paste from GitHub API output); (c) whether the coverage ledger artifact already exists and its schema; (d) the current allowlist contents. This converts unverified assertions into confirmed starting state for the executor.

findings.location: SC1 ('a single always-runs required check review-gate'), SC3 ('the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap… reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset'), SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml')
findings.finding:
Two potential over-engineering signals are present in the plan text, though both require codebase confirmation:
1. The plan introduces a new 'review-gate' required check as a wrapper/aggregator over what appears to be at least three existing CI workflows (review-coverage-invariant, dangling-references, ruleset-invariants) plus a separate 'llm-review' check. If GitHub's native required-status-checks can simply require the existing checks directly, a new aggregator check is an extra indirection layer used at one call-site (Rule-of-Three flag). However, if the aggregator provides fail-closed semantics that the individual checks cannot, it is justified — this cannot be determined from the plan text alone.
2. The plan maintains a 'required-checks.txt' file as a file-system mirror of the live GitHub ruleset ('reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt AND the live ruleset'). This dual-source-of-truth pattern (file + live API) adds a synchronization surface that could itself drift. If the ruleset-invariants.yml check already guards against drift, this may be a circular dependency rather than a genuine safety net.
findings.suggested_edit: Clarify in the plan: (a) why 'review-gate' must be a new aggregator check rather than adding the existing checks directly to the ruleset's required list; (b) whether required-checks.txt is the authoritative source or merely a cache — and if the latter, whether it is truly necessary given the ruleset-invariants guard already exists.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA section (SC1–SC6) and SEQUENCING paragraph
findings.finding: Each SC names an experiment/verification label (E2/E13, V0.3a-post-fix, E6', V0.11, E5/E8/E14/E15, E11/E-compute-hash) as the proof of done, but the plan text never defines what those experiments actually assert, what their pass/fail output looks like, or where the completion-verifier runs. Without knowing what 'E2 PASS' means in observable terms, it is impossible for a reviewer (or the executing agent) to confirm terminal state has been reached. The epic-level 'completion-verifier PASS' gate mentioned in the SC preamble is similarly undefined — no script path, no checklist, no owner. Additionally, SC3 mentions a 'required-checks.txt' file that must match the live ruleset, but no story or step explicitly describes generating or updating that file, leaving a gap between the enforcement swap and the durable artifact that proves it.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add one concrete observable: the exact command/UI path that produces a green/red signal and the expected value (e.g., 'run ./scripts/verify-gate.sh; exit 0 means pass'). Define the completion-verifier as a named script or checklist. Add a step in story 6 or 7 that regenerates required-checks.txt and commits it so SC3's 'reflected in required-checks.txt' is actionable.

findings.location: SEQUENCING paragraph and stories 1–8 references throughout

SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'), SC3 ('merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap'), SC5 ('review-gate allowlist'), NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT
findings.finding: The plan names three specific YAML workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) and implies a new `review-gate` check replaces `merge-pipeline-checks` as a required check, plus an allowlist governing which paths are exempt. However: (1) it is not confirmed these file names actually exist in the repository; (2) no symbols, job names, or step names within those files are cited, making it ambiguous which jobs must be patched for fail-closed behaviour; (3) the 'review-gate allowlist' referenced in SC5 is not named as a file path or config key anywhere — it is unclear whether this is a step-level path filter, a separate config file, or a GitHub ruleset path filter; (4) the 'required-checks.txt' artefact mentioned in SC3 has no declared home in the repo. Without codebase access these cannot be confirmed, but their absence from the plan raises rework risk.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Files/symbols touched' table to the epic (or each story) listing: exact workflow file paths and job IDs to be modified; the allowlist config file path and its format; the location of required-checks.txt. Mark any that are net-new. Doing this removes ambiguity about blast radius before execution begins.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('warn-first' + 'enforce-flip') vs. NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SEQUENCING ('story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified') vs. SC2/SC3 phrasing; SC5 vs. SC1
findings.finding: Three potential internal tensions are present: (1) The NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT says 'every commit reaching main must pass an LLM review that is ruleset-enforced' (present tense, absolute), while DELIVERY PRINCIPLES say enforcement only lands in the final story (story 7). If any story 1–6 merges to main in warn mode, the NON-NEGOTIABLE is technically violated during that window. This is arguably intended and managed, but the plan never explicitly resolves the tension (e.g., 'warn mode is acceptable on feature branches; only main-targeting PRs are gated post-story-7'). (2) SC1 says 'no code_changed skip', while SC4 exempts the 'event-sourced ticket store' and SC5 exempts 'images, lockfiles, generated'. These exemptions are path-based, not code_changed-based, but the relationship between the 'no skip' rule in SC1 and the exemptions in SC4/SC5 is never reconciled — if the exemptions are implemented as an 'always-runs but exits-0 on exempt paths' pattern vs. a 'skip entirely' pattern, those have different ruleset semantics and one approach may silently violate SC1. (3) SEQUENCING says stories 1–4 'may proceed in parallel after start', but SC2 requires all three carrier YAMLs to be fail-closed simultaneously ('ALL THREE'), implying those stories must coordinate their enforce-flips atomically. If each story owns one YAML, parallel independent merges may leave SC2 in a partial state temporarily.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a clarifying note under DELIVERY PRINCIPLES explicitly stating that the NON-NEGOTIABLE applies post-story-7 and that warn-mode stories targeting main are exempted during the rollout window. Reconcile SC1 ('no skip') with SC4/SC5 exemptions by specifying the implementation pattern (e.g., 'check always runs but exits-0 for exempt paths — this satisfies SC1 because the check is required and always present'). Note in the story 2/3 area that the three carrier YAML fail-closed changes must be merged as a coordinated batch or within a single story to satisfy SC2 atomically.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6 and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub')
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria require live GitHub operations that cannot be completed within a single agent session without persistent real-infrastructure access. SC1 requires verifying 'an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED (not skip-to-success)' on live GitHub. SC2 requires triggering actual CI runs with scope-stripped/expired tokens across three live workflows. SC3 requires confirming the live main ruleset retains both checks after the swap. SC6 requires real PR1 title and sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle observations. These are runtime/environment assertions, not code or config checks. Additionally, no criterion is expressed with a measurable threshold (e.g., exit codes, specific log lines, API response fields); they are described in narrative form ('BLOCKED', 'RED', 'fail-closes') without specifying the exact observable signal an agent should check. The warn-mode/enforce-flip sequencing also implies a multi-session, multi-PR-traffic validation window that is fundamentally out-of-session.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC, add a concrete, in-session-verifiable assertion: e.g., 'CI exits non-zero when GITHUB_TOKEN is replaced with a scope-stripped token (verified by `act` dry-run or unit test of the wrapper script)' rather than 'makes the gate RED on live GitHub'. Separate live-GitHub smoke-test steps into a clearly labeled post-merge verification checklist so the agent's in-session deliverables are distinct from human/ops validation steps.

findings.location: SEQUENCING section vs. SUCCESS CRITERIA; SC5 ('behavior-bearing surfaces') vs. plan body; 'stories 1-4' referenced but story content not present
findings.finding: The plan references 'stories 1-4', 'story 5', etc. and named experiments (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13–E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, R8) but the story content and experiment definitions are not reproduced in this plan—they live in external docs (docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020). Without access to those documents it is impossible to verify that every SC maps 1:1 to a described story, or that no story is orphaned. SC5 mentions 'behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config)' and 'event-sourced ticket store' (SC4) but neither term is defined in the plan body, creating potential terminology drift. SC6 mentions 'descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle' as UX fixes—these are not connected to any SC metric, making them unchecked described work. No duplicate SCs were detected, but the external-doc dependency makes full coherence verification impossible.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the story summaries and experiment definitions here, or add a cross-reference table mapping each SC/experiment ID to the specific section/story in the external docs. Define 'behavior-bearing surfaces', 'event-sourced ticket store', and 'coverage ledger' in a glossary section within the plan. Promote SC6's UX items (PR1 titles, sprint lifecycle) to a measurable sub-criterion.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT and DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
Title vs. plan body and SUCCESS CRITERIA
SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC4; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story'); named experiments E2/E5/E8/E13/E14/E15/V0.3a-post-fix/V0.11/R8
findings.finding: The plan's testing strategy relies almost entirely on live-GitHub experiment references (E-series/V-series) whose content is external and not reproducible by the agent. There is no description of: (a) unit or integration tests for the fail-closed wrapper logic (exit-code behavior on token failure); (b) RED-before-GREEN ordering for the coverage-ledger logic; (c) boundary/failure-path cases expressed as runnable assertions—e.g., what happens when origin/main..head is empty, when a SHA appears in the ledger but with a mismatched timestamp, or when the ruleset API call times out. SC4's 'coverage ledger warm-hits across re-runs (no flaky-check team block)' is a performance/reliability claim with no stated test methodology. SC2 names three carrier workflows but does not specify what test harness exercises them under the token-failure condition. Without inline test descriptions an executor cannot build passing tests before writing production code.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a Testing sub-section per story (or per SC) with: (1) a unit test spec for each fail-closed wrapper asserting non-zero exit on scope-stripped/expired token; (2) a contract test for the coverage ledger (warm-hit, cold-miss, empty-range edge cases); (3) a local simulation step (e.g., `act`, mock GitHub API, or shell script with faked env vars) that can be run in-session before the live-GitHub smoke test; (4) explicit RED-before-GREEN steps for the review-gate check itself.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SC2 ('ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'); SC3 ('live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'); SC4 ('coverage ledger warm-hits')
findings.finding: The plan asserts several facts about the current codebase/environment state that an executor must verify before proceeding: (1) That the three named workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) already exist and already have fail-closed token-check logic to be hardened—if any are absent or structured differently, SC2's testing approach breaks. (2) That a live 'main' ruleset already exists with 'llm-review' as a required check—SC3's swap instruction presumes this. (3) That a 'coverage ledger' mechanism exists and has warm-hit semantics—SC4 presumes it. (4) That 'required-checks.txt' exists as the file-based source of truth referenced in SC3. (5) That experiments V0.3a-post-fix and V0.11 were previously run and their outcomes are available as baselines—the plan references them as verification targets but does not say what was observed. None of these are verified within the plan text; all require codebase/environment inspection.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Current-state baseline' section that explicitly states: which files/configs exist today (with file paths), what the live ruleset currently contains (paste required-checks list), whether the coverage ledger is implemented and where, and what experiments V0.3a-post-fix and V0.11 recorded. Mark each as 'verified at plan-write time: YES/NO' so an executor knows what to re-confirm on checkout.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA: SC1 ('Verified live: an allowlisted-only PR2 carrying un-reviewed content is BLOCKED'), SC2 ('Verified: a scope-stripped/expired token makes the gate RED'), SC3 ('the merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap is reflected on BOTH required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'), SC4 ('no flaky-check team block'), SC6 ('docs reflect the final enforced architecture')
findings.finding: Multiple acceptance criteria require live GitHub operations (live ruleset reads/writes, real PR2 traffic, non-admin PAT containment reads, admin PAT provisioning, R8 live-parity guard) that cannot be completed, simulated, or verified inside a single agent working session without human intervention or real repository access. SC1 and SC2 each require creating an actual PR2 with specific token conditions and observing real GitHub gate behavior. SC3 requires both writing to and reading from a live GitHub ruleset. SC6 requires docs to 'reflect' an as-executed outcome that only exists post-execution. None of these have a defined offline simulation path ('provided auth' is mentioned in DELIVERY PRINCIPLES but no mock/stub strategy is specified). The plan's own DELIVERY PRINCIPLES acknowledge 'Live-verify each story on real GitHub' — confirming these are out-of-session for an agent without live repo access.
findings.suggested_edit: For each SC that requires live validation, add a clearly scoped fallback verifier (e.g., a dry-run mode output, a fixture-based integration test, or a recorded HTTP interaction) that an agent can execute without live GitHub access. Separate the 'live smoke test' step (human/CI task) from the AC the agent is responsible for completing.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA vs. SEQUENCING section; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('E-series/V-series experiments'); SC1 [E2/E13], SC2 [V0.3a-post-fix, R8 fail-closed], SC3 [E6', V0.11], SC4 [E5/E8/E14/E15], SC5 [E11/E-compute-hash]
findings.finding: Every SC references external experiment/review IDs (E2, E13, V0.3a-post-fix, R8, E6', V0.11, E-compute-hash, etc.) that are not defined anywhere in the visible plan text; they appear to live exclusively in the referenced source documents (option-a-pivot-plan.md, ADR-0020). An agent cannot confirm whether all referenced experiments have a corresponding criterion, or vice versa, without access to those documents. Additionally, the SEQUENCING section describes story-level dependency links ('story 5 depends on 1+2+4') but no stories are listed in the visible plan body — this is described work with no AC. SC6 ('UX correctness fixes land') is partially described in DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('descriptive PR1 titles; sprint draft-umbrella lifecycle') but has no dedicated measurable acceptance criterion with a verifiable pass/fail condition.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the relevant experiment definitions (at least a one-sentence gloss) so the plan is self-contained, or add a glossary/appendix. Add at least one measurable AC for SC6's UX fixes (e.g., 'PR1 title template is present in .github/ and used by the flow script'). List the stories explicitly if sequencing is a first-class concern.

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT; SUCCESS CRITERIA preamble; DELIVERY PRINCIPLES
Title vs. plan body overall
findings.finding: The plan body is coherent with the title. The NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT, DELIVERY PRINCIPLES, SUCCESS CRITERIA, and SEQUENCING sections all consistently describe hardening the two-tier flow into a ruleset-enforced, fail-closed review gate. No significant scope drift (adding unrelated features) or scope under-delivery (silently omitting title-promised elements) is detectable. The warn-first / enforce-flip pattern is internally consistent throughout.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA (all SCs); DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('Live-verify each story on real GitHub… AND by the named E-series/V-series experiments')
findings.finding: The testing plan is almost entirely expressed as 'verified live on real GitHub' with references to external experiment IDs (E2, E5, E6', E8, E11, E13, E14, E15, V0.3a-post-fix, V0.11, E-compute-hash) whose content is not visible in the plan. There is no description of: (a) what test artifacts/scripts will be authored, (b) how failure-path tests (expired token, stripped scope, un-reviewed SHA) are injected and asserted, (c) RED-before-GREEN ordering for the fail-closed checks, (d) boundary/edge cases (e.g., a PR with exactly zero changed files in scope, or a PR whose head SHA equals a previously reviewed SHA). The plan mentions 'no false wedge' (SC4) as an outcome but gives no test procedure for verifying it beyond vague experiment references. Without the experiment definitions, an agent cannot reproduce the test.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Test Plan' subsection to each story (or to the epic itself) that lists: the script/fixture that drives each scenario, the expected stdout/exit-code, and whether a real PR or a locally-reproducible fixture is required. At minimum, describe the failure-path injection method (e.g., 'set GITHUB_TOKEN to a token with no repo scope; assert exit code 1 and stderr contains "token scope insufficient"').

findings.location: NON-NEGOTIABLE REQUIREMENT ('every commit reaching main must pass an LLM review that is ruleset-enforced'); SC2 ('all review-enforcing CI wrappers fail-closed… in ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml'); SC3 ('live main ruleset requires review-gate and retains llm-review'); DELIVERY PRINCIPLES ('admin PAT: provision/read; non-admin: containment reads')
findings.finding: The plan asserts the existence of several specific artifacts without confirming they already exist in the codebase: (1) three named workflow files (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml) are assumed present and already structured to accept a fail-closed mode — if any are absent or differently structured, the SC2 requirement breaks; (2) a live 'main' ruleset on GitHub is assumed to already require 'llm-review' and to be modifiable to add 'review-gate'; (3) 'required-checks.txt' is assumed to exist and be the authoritative source-of-truth file; (4) the 'event-sourced ticket store' exemption in SC4 assumes the ticket store path is already defined in the allowlist config. None of these are verified in the plan text, and all are cited as direct inputs to the implementation. Additionally, the plan references docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md (CONVERGED) and ADR-0020 as source-of-truth — if those documents are stale or inconsistent with each other or the live repo, the plan will silently build on wrong foundations.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Prerequisites / pre-flight checks' section that lists each assumed artifact (the three YMLs, the live ruleset, required-checks.txt, the ticket-store path, the PAT scopes) with a one-line verification command (e.g., 'gh ruleset list --repo <repo> | grep main', 'ls .github/workflows/review-coverage-invariant.yml'). Mark these as MUST-VERIFY before story 1 starts.

findings.location: Plan description §SEQUENCING + success criteria SC1–SC6 + individual story references
findings.finding: The plan refers repeatedly to concrete artefacts, experiments, and stories (story 1–8, E2/E5/E6'/E8/E11/E13/E14/E15, V0.1–V0.12, R8, ADR-0020, required-checks.txt, review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml, the 'enforce-flip' runbook, the 'coverage ledger', the 'two-tier flow') without defining or inlining any of them. An executing agent must follow these references to know what to implement. For example: (a) what exactly is 'story 5' / 'story 7'? (b) what does the 'enforce-flip' runbook say? (c) what constitutes 'per-SHA coverage' in the ledger, and what is the ledger's storage format? (d) what is the 'event-sourced ticket store' path pattern used by the allowlist exemption? (e) what PAT scope is 'scope-stripped'? The plan's own source of truth is listed as docs/handoff/option-a-pivot-plan.md and docs/adr/0020-two-tier-hardening-over-merge-queue.md — both external documents not reproduced here. If those documents are unavailable to the agent, it will be forced to stop and clarify on nearly every concrete implementation step.
findings.suggested_edit: Either inline the per-story implementation specs (at minimum: file/symbol targets, algorithm for per-SHA coverage, allowlist pattern for ticket-store exemption, enforce-flip runbook summary) into this ticket, or confirm that the referenced external documents are guaranteed to be on the agent's context path before execution begins.

findings.location: SUCCESS CRITERIA SC1–SC6 + SEQUENCING section
SEQUENCING section: 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel … story 5 depends on 1+2+4 … story 6 depends on 5 … story 7 depends on 1-6 ALL verified … story 8 is independent'
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges 8 distinct stories with an explicit dependency DAG, yet it is presented as a single ticket/epic rather than decomposed into child tickets. For an epic this is fine in principle, but the plan provides zero per-story acceptance criteria, no per-story scope, and no per-story verification step — only the top-level SC1–SC6. This means an agent given this single ticket cannot know what 'done' means for any individual story, cannot safely ship partial progress, and cannot isolate failures. The plan also mixes horizontal concerns (CI yaml authoring, ruleset API calls, ledger storage, UX copy changes) into an undifferentiated blob, violating the stated delivery principle of 'warn-first per story'. Story 8 ('UX') is explicitly called out as independent but is not separately deliverable as written.
findings.suggested_edit: Create one child ticket per story (1–8) with: (a) its own file/symbol targets, (b) its own warn-mode vs enforce-mode acceptance criteria, (c) its own named COURSE-CORRECTION TRIGGER (the plan mentions this concept but it only appears in the parent). The parent epic retains SC1–SC6 as rollup success criteria.

findings.location: SC2: 'ALL THREE carriers — review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml' + SC3: 'review-gate' as a new required check on top of 'llm-review'
findings.finding: The plan introduces at least four distinct GitHub Actions workflow files as enforcement carriers (review-coverage-invariant.yml, dangling-references.yml, ruleset-invariants.yml, plus the new review-gate check), each of which must independently implement fail-closed token/scope logic. Without seeing the live codebase it is ambiguous whether the shared fail-closed wrapper is a single reusable composite action or replicated across each file. If replicated, this is a classic Rule-of-Three violation — any future token scope change must be patched in four places. The plan does not mention a shared composite action or shared shell library for this logic.
findings.suggested_edit: Consider extracting the fail-closed precondition/token/expiry check into a single reusable composite action (e.g. .github/actions/fail-closed-gate/action.yml) called by all three carrier workflows. This ensures one patch point for future token scope changes and makes the anti-slop concern moot.

findings.location: SC3: 'required-checks.txt and the live ruleset'; SC5: 'behavior-bearing surfaces (docs/**, skills, behavior-driving config) are removed from the review-gate allowlist'; SEQUENCING: references to 'merge-pipeline-checks->review-gate swap'
findings.finding: The plan names several target artefacts (required-checks.txt, review-gate allowlist, ruleset-invariants.yml, the live GitHub ruleset) and implies edits to docs/**, skill files, and behavior-driving config, but does not enumerate specific file paths, GitHub ruleset IDs, or the current allowlist entries. It is impossible to verify from this plan alone whether the named files exist, what their current state is, or whether there are additional callers of the existing 'merge-pipeline-checks' required check that would break when it is swapped to 'review-gate'. The blast radius of the ruleset swap (any branch protection rule, any repo-level required-status-check config) is acknowledged conceptually ('R8 RED is repo-wide') but not mapped to specific consumers.
findings.suggested_edit: Add a 'Files/Symbols to Touch' section listing: (1) exact workflow file paths being modified, (2) current allowlist entries and their proposed replacement, (3) the GitHub ruleset ID or name being edited, (4) any branch protection rules that reference 'merge-pipeline-checks' by name and must be updated simultaneously.

findings.location: DELIVERY PRINCIPLES 'warn-first' vs SC2 'under enforce mode' + SEQUENCING 'stories 1-4 may proceed in parallel' vs SC2 requiring all three carriers to be fixed
findings.finding: Two internal tensions: (1) The DELIVERY PRINCIPLES state 'every story ships its enforcing logic in warn/advisory mode … BEFORE any enforce-flip', implying warn-mode is the default state through story 6.
